“ I am he who carries food to Eana, I am he who brought the wedding gifts to Unug, 
“ I am he who kisses the holy lips, he who dances on the holy knees,
“ The knees of Inanna!


“ Please change my hands into gazelle hands, 
“ Change my feet into gazelle feet, so I can evade my demons. 
“ Let me escape with my life to Kubiresh-dil-daresh." 


Utu accepted his tears. Like a merciful man, he showed him mercy. He changed his hands into gazelle hands. He changed his feet into gazelle feet.


So, Dumuzi evaded the demons. He escaped with his life to Kubiresh-dil-daresh. 


The demons searched for him, but did not find him.


They caught Dumuzi at Kubiresh.The men surrounded him and drained the standing waters. They twisted a cord for him, they knotted a net for him. They wove a reed hawser for him, and they cut sticks for him. The one in front of him threw missiles at him, the one behind him beat his ribs. His hands were bound in handcuffs, his arms were bound in fetters. 


The lad raised his hands heavenward to Utu: 


" Utu, you are my brother-in-law, I am your sister's husband! 
“ I am he who carries food to Eana, I am he who brought the wedding gifts to Unug, 
“ I am he who kisses the holy lips, I am he who dances on the holy knees, 
“ The knees of Inanna!


“ Please change my hands into gazelle  hands, 
“ Change my feet into gazelle  feet, 
“ I can escape to the house of old woman Belili." 


Utu accepted his tears. He changed his hands into gazelle hands. He changed his feet into gazelle feet. So he evaded the demons and escaped with his life to the house of Belili. 


He approached the house, crying:


" Old woman! 
“ I am not just a man,  I am the husband of a goddess, 


“ Would you pour water…please…so I can drink water? 
“ Would you sprinkle flour..please…so I can eat grain?"


She poured water, and she sprinkled flour, and he sat down inside the house. When the old woman left the house, the demons saw her.


They approached, saying:        


" Unless the old woman is aware of Dumuzi's whereabouts, 
“ She is indeed looking frightened, she is indeed screaming in a frightened way, 


“ Come, let us go to the house!" 


They caught Dumuzi at the house of old woman Belili. The men surrounded him. They twisted a cord for him, they knotted a net for him. They wove a reed hawser for him, and they cut sticks for him. The one in front of him threw missiles at him, the one behind him poked his heels. His hands were bound in handcuffs, his arms were bound in fetters. 


The lad raised his hands heavenward to Utu: 


" Utu, you are my brother-in-law, I am your sister's husband,
“ I am he who carries food to E-ana, I am he who brought the wedding gifts to Unug,
“ I am he who kisses the holy lips, and I am he who dances on the holy knees, 
“ The knees of Inanna!


“ Please change my hands into gazelle hands, 
“ Change my feet into gazelle feet, 
“ I can escape to the holy sheepfold, my sister's sheepfold." 


Utu accepted his tears. He changed his hands into gazelle hands, he changed his feet into gazelle feet, so he evaded the demons, and escaped with his life. He approached the holy sheepfold, his sister's sheepfold.


Geshtinanna cried toward heaven, cried toward earth. Her cries covered the horizon completely like a cloth, they were spread out like linen. She lacerated her eyes, she lacerated her face, she lacerated her ears in public. In private, she lacerated her buttocks.  


Geshtinanna cried:


" My brother, I will go round in the streets, and I will mourn my king" 


But, Geshtinanna saw the demons return to the city. She knew that her brother must still be alive at the sight of them.


The demons said: 


" Unless Geshtinanna is aware of Dumuzi's whereabouts, 
“ She is indeed looking frightened, she is indeed screaming in a frightened way! 


“ Come, let us go to the sheepfold and cow-pen." 


When the first demon entered the sheepfold and cow-pen, he set fire to the bolt. When the second entered the sheepfold and cow-pen, he set fire to the shepherd's stick. When the third entered the sheepfold and cow-pen, he removed the cover of the holy churn. When the fourth entered the sheepfold and cow-pen, he poured water on my holy brazier. When the fifth demon entered the sheepfold and cow-pen, he tore down my holy drinking cup from the peg where it hung. When the sixth demon entered the sheepfold and cow-pen, the churns lay on their side, and no milk was poured. When the seventh demon entered the sheepfold and cow-pen, the drinking cups lay on their side.


 Dumuzi was dead, the sheepfold was haunted.


He woke up; it was a dream! 


He shivered; it was sleep! 


He rubbed his eyes. 


He was terrified.


Daughter of Heaven:
Tablet I: Inanna takes the E-ana:


Holy Inana; sister of the hero, Youthful Utu, came down to greet her brother. Before dawn, Inanna came down to Utu. At the dead of night, she had plan to seize the E-ana, An’s great temple. The lady Inana had set her mind to capturing the great heavens.


Holy Inana spoke to her brother the hero, Youthful Utu: 


" My brother, I want to tell you something, pay attention to my speech.
“ My brother, Utu, my twin, I want to tell you something 
“ Pay attention to my speech!"


Utu, answered holy Inana: 


" My sister, I swear an oath by the life of heaven, 
“ I swear by the life of the rainbow of heaven, 
“ My twin sister, Holy Inana, 
“ I swear by the life of my throne, by my majesty: 
“ I will follow what my sister says to me, I will follow what Holy Inana says to me."


The maiden, Inana, answered her brother the hero: 


" Dumuzi, my spouse, has made love to me, and has kissed me. 
“ I wanted proper kingship for him, 
“ But Father An, he did not give it to him. 


“ I fashioned with him, a deal, and he took pleasure in the fruit of my body.
“ But majestic An would not give him E-ana. 


“ The heavens are ours, the earth is ours:
“ E-ana should be captured from An


“ After you have taken thought, listen to what I say to you.
“ Examine these words for me, you must observe these instructions: 


“Raise up the evil wind, the south wind, against me."


From the heavens, the great fisherman came. From the heavens, the holy barge came. Holy Inana embarked on the barge, the Barge of Heaven. 


The rope was let loose. The south wind rose up, the evil wind. That evil wind rose up. 


In the distant heavens, sent forth from the hands of Hienhi-sag, the evil south wind rose up. The fisherman guided the barge through the tall-growing reed thickets.


Inana spoke to Adagbir: 


" Youthful Utu shall guide the barge
“ He shall guide the barge on the south wind


“ Youthful Utu shall guide the barge, and I shall lead the way” 


Adagbir answered Holy Inana:


" My lady, you cannot do this deed, your divinity will be tarnished.
“ I, The fisherman, will be killed by that south wind. 
“  My lady, if you travel on the barge, and he raises the south wind,
“ If he raises that evil wind, barges and small boats will sink in the marshes”




Holy Inana answered the fisherman: 


 " If you are to find the temple E-ana, we must


“ I am to gaze in admiration at that place;
“ And, you said you would sail the narrow passage."




Adagbir answered holy Inana:


" There through the reed thickets and the tall-growing reeds.
“ For you I shall sail, to find E-ana, which came forth from heaven."


Adagbir, Fisherman of Enlil, guided the barge through the reed thickets and the tall-growing reeds. He gazed in admiration at E-ana which had come forth from heaven. Cul-a-zida, An's herdsman, grasped the cosmic tethering rope in his hands. After he had brought the E-ana forth from the sky, he overcame the protective deities. He steadied the rope and kept it below the horizon.


Having drunk cleansing water from the Ulaya river, Inana stamped on the scorpion and cut off its tail. Like a lion it bellowed in an angry roar, but its cries died down. She seized it, and  she threw it in her path, to make it secure. 


Having heard the dying cries, the guards poured forth from the temple. The clay of creation was brought forth and laid out in offering to Inana.


“ I refuse,”


The Great Lady of Heaven delivered those words to An.


Having heard those words, An slapped his thighs in exasperation, his voice filled with sighs of grief: 


" What has my child done? 
“ She has become greater than me! 
“ What has Inanna done? 


“ From now on, the normal length of daylight becomes shorter, 
“ Daylight converts to night-time from today, when the day's watch is three units long, 


“ Daylight shall be equal to night-time."


And now, when day began, it was indeed so.


An, who created gods and humankind, gazed at Holy Inana and addressed the favourite wife, who travels by his side, unable to describe this arrogance. 


Flustered, he spoke to Inana: 


" My child, your intentions you did not say. 


“ You were able to capture E-ana! 


“ Inana, E-ana should be as firm as heaven, it should not be toppled.
“ Its name should be "The Settlement of the Land" , it should have no rival
“ Mankind, all of the people, should prostrate themselves at her feet." 


And now, under that sun and on that day, it was indeed so.


She had captured E-ana from An. She secured it and retired it back to heaven. Now, Inana speaks of the E-ana as the house that is the place of the lady. The goddess who has attained her triumphant position, declared her glory in the good place, having captured E-ana from An.


Tablet II: Inanna's descent to Erkalla:


From the great heaven, the goddess, Inana, set her mind on the great below. The mistress abandoned heaven, abandoned earth, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the office of en, abandoned the office of lagar, and descended to the underworld. 


She abandoned the E-ana in Unug, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the E-muc-kalama in Bad-tibira, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the Giguna in Zabalam, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the E-cara in Adab, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the Barag-dur-jara in Nibru, and descended to the underworld. 


She abandoned the Hursaj-kalama in Kic, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the E-Ulmac in Agade, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the Ibgal in Umma, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the E-Dilmuna in Urim, and descended to the underworld. 


She abandoned the Amac-e-kug in Kisiga, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the E-ecdam-kug in Jirsu, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the E-sig-mece-du in Isin, and descended to the underworld. 


She abandoned the Anzagar in Akcak, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the Nijin-jar-kug in Curuppag, and descended to the underworld. She abandoned the E-cag-hula in Kazallu, and descended to the underworld.


She took the seven divine powers. She collected the divine powers and grasped them in her hand. With the good divine powers, she went on her way. She put a turban, headgear for the open country, on her head. She took a wig for her forehead. She hung small lapis-lazuli beads around her neck.


She placed twin egg-shaped beads on her breast. She covered her body with a pala dress, the garment of ladyship. She placed mascara which is called he-em-du he-em-du, "Let a man come, let him come", on her eyes. 


She pulled the pectoral, which is called ja-nu ja-nu,  "Come, man, come", over her breast. She placed a golden ring on her hand. She held the lapis-lazuli measuring rod and measuring line in her hand.


Inana travelled towards the underworld. Her minister Nincubura travelled behind her. Holy Inana said to Nincubura: 


" Come my faithful minister of E-ana, 
“ My minister who speaks fair words, who speaks trustworthy words
“ I am going to give you instructions
“ I am going to say something to you, let you heed my words


" On this day I will descend to the underworld. 
“ When I have arrived in the underworld, make a lament for me on the ruin mounds.
“ Beat the drum for me in the sanctuary.
“ Make the rounds of the houses of the gods for me.
" Lacerate your eyes for me,
“ Lacerate your nose for me. 
“ Lacerate your ears for me, in public.
“ In private, lacerate your buttocks for me.
“ Like a pauper, clothe yourself in a single garment 
“ And all alone set your foot in the E-kur, the house of Enlil.


" When you have entered the E-kur, the house of Enlil,
“ Lament before Enlil and speak:


“" Father Enlil, don't let anyone kill your daughter in the underworld.
“” Don't let your precious metal be alloyed there with the dirt of the underworld.
“” Don't let your precious lapis lazuli be split there with the mason's stone.
“” Don't let your boxwood be chopped up there with the carpenter's wood.
“” Don't let young lady Inana be killed in the underworld."”


" If Enlil does not help you in this matter, go to Urim.
“ In the E-mud-kura at Urim,
“ When you have entered the E-kic-nu-jal, the house of Nanna,
“ Lament before Nanna and say: 


"” Father Nanna, don't let anyone kill your daughter in the underworld.
“” Don't let your precious metal be alloyed there with the dirt of the underworld.
“” Don't let your precious lapis lazuli be split there with the mason's stone.
“” Don't let your boxwood be chopped up there with the carpenter's wood.
“” Don't let young lady Inana be killed in the underworld."”


" And if Nanna does not help you in this matter, go to Eridug.
“ In Eridug, when you have entered the house of Enki,
“ Lament before Enki and repeat:


“” Father Enki, don't let anyone kill your daughter in the underworld.
““ Don't let your precious metal be alloyed there with the dirt of the underworld
“” Don't let your precious lapis lazuli be split there with the mason's stone.
“” Don't let your boxwood be chopped up there with the carpenter's wood.
“” Don't let young lady Inana be killed in the underworld."


" Father Enki, the lord of great wisdom, 
“ Knows about the life-giving plant and the life-giving water.
“ He is the one who will restore me to life."


When Inana travelled on towards the underworld, her minister Nincubura travelled on behind her. She said to her minister: 


" Go now, my Nincubura, and pay attention. 
“ Do not neglect the instructions I gave you."


When Inana arrived at the palace, Ganzer, she pushed aggressively on the door of the underworld. She shouted aggressively at the gate of the underworld: 


" Open up, doorman, open up.
“ Open up, Neti, open up
“ I am all alone and I want to come in."


Neti, the chief doorman of the underworld, answered Holy Inana: 


" Who are you?" 


" I am Inana, going to the east." 


" If you are Inana going to the east,
“ Why have you travelled to the land of no return?
“ How did you set your heart on the road whose traveller never returns?"


Holy Inana answered him: 


" Because lord Gud-gal-ana,
” The husband of my elder sister, holy Erec-ki-gala, has died;
“ In order to have his funeral rites observed,
“ She offers generous libations at his wake,
“ That is the reason."


Neti, the chief doorman of the underworld, answered holy Inana: 


" Stay here, Inana.
“ I will speak to my mistress, Erec-ki-gala
“ And tell her what you have said."


Neti, the chief doorman of the underworld, entered the house of his mistress, Erec-ki-gala. 


He said:


" My mistress, there is a lone girl outside.
“ It is Inana, your sister,
“ She has arrived at the Palace Ganzer.
“ She pushed aggressively on the door of the underworld.
“ She shouted aggressively at the gate of the underworld.
“ She has abandoned E-ana and has descended to the underworld.
“ She has taken the seven divine powers. 
“ She has collected the divine powers and grasped them in her hand.
“ She has come on her way with all the good divine powers.
“ She has put a turban, headgear for the open country, on her head.
“ She has taken a wig for her forehead.
“ She has hung small lapis-lazuli beads around her neck.
" She has placed twin egg-shaped beads on her breast. 
“ She has covered her body with the pala dress of ladyship.
“ She has placed mascara on her eyes.
“ She h        as pulled the pectoral over her breast.
“ She has placed a golden ring on her hand.
“ She is holding the lapis-lazuli measuring rod and measuring line in her hand."


When she heard this, Erec-ki-gala slapped the side of her thigh. She bit her lip and took the words to heart. She said to Neti, her chief doorman: 


" Come Neti, my chief doorman of the underworld,
“ Don't neglect the instructions I will give you.
“ Let the seven gates of the underworld be bolted.
“ Then let each door of the Palace Ganzer be opened separately.
“ As for her, after she has entered, and crouched down and had her clothes removed,
“ They will be carried away."


Neti, the chief doorman of the underworld, paid attention to the instructions of his mistress. He bolted the seven gates of the underworld. Then he opened each of the doors of the Palace Ganzer separately. He said to Holy Inana: 


" Come on, Inana, and enter."


And when Inana entered, when she entered the first gate, the turban, headgear for the open country, was removed from her head.


" What is this?" 


" Be satisfied, Inana,
“ A divine power of the underworld has been fulfilled.
“ Inana, you must not open your mouth against the rites of the underworld."


When she entered the second gate, the small lapis-lazuli beads were removed from her neck. 


" What is this?" 


“ Be satisfied, Inana, 
“ A divine power of the underworld has been fulfilled.
“ Inana, you must not open your mouth against the rites of the underworld."


When she entered the third gate, the twin egg-shaped beads were removed from her breast.


" What is this?” 


“ Be satisfied, Inana, 
“ A divine power of the underworld has been fulfilled.
“ Inana, you must not open your mouth against the rites of the underworld."


When she entered the fourth gate, the "Come, man, come" pectoral was removed from her breast.


" What is this?" 


“ Be satisfied, Inana, 
“ A divine power of the underworld has been fulfilled.
“ Inana, you must not open your mouth against the rites of the underworld."


When she entered the fifth gate, the golden ring was removed from her hand. 


" What is this?" 


“ Be satisfied, Inana, 
“ A divine power of the underworld has been fulfilled.
“ Inana, you must not open your mouth against the rites of the underworld."


When she entered the sixth gate, the lapis-lazuli measuring rod and measuring line were removed from her hand.


" What is this?" 


“ Be satisfied, Inana, 
“ A divine power of the underworld has been fulfilled.
“ Inana, you must not open your mouth against the rites of the underworld."


When she entered the seventh gate, the pala dress, the garment of ladyship, was removed from her body. 


" What is this?" 


“ Be satisfied, Inana, 
“ A divine power of the underworld has been fulfilled.
“ Inana, you must not open your mouth against the rites of the underworld."


After she had crouched down and had her clothes removed, they were carried away. Then she made her sister Erec-ki-gala rise from her throne. As Erec-ki-gala rose, Inana sat on her throne. The Anuna, the seven judges, rendered their decision against her. 


They looked at her. It was the look of death. They spoke to her. It was the speech of anger. They shouted at her. It was the shout of heavy guilt. The afflicted woman was turned into a corpse, and the corpse was hung on a hook.


Tablet III: 


After three days and three nights had passed, her minister Nincubura carried out the instructions of her mistress. She did not forget her orders. She did not neglect her instructions.


She made a lament for her in her ruined houses. She beat the drum for her in the sanctuaries. She made the rounds of the houses of the gods for her. 


She lacerated her eyes for her, she lacerated her nose. In private she lacerated her buttocks for her. Like a pauper, she clothed herself in a single garment. All alone she set her foot in the E-kur, the house of Enlil.


When she had entered the E-kur, the house of Enlil, she lamented before him: 


" Father Enlil, don't let anyone kill your daughter in the underworld.
“ Don't let your precious metal be alloyed there with the dirt of the underworld.
“ Don't let your precious lapis lazuli be split there with the mason's stone.
“ Don't let your boxwood be chopped up there with the carpenter's wood.
“ Don't let young lady Inana be killed in the underworld."


In his rage, Father Enlil answered Nincubura: 


“ Inana craved the great heaven and she craved the great below as well.
“ The divine powers of the underworld are divine powers which should not be craved,
“ For whoever gets them must remain in the underworld.
“ Who, having got to that place, could then expect to come up again?"


Thus Father Enlil did not help in this matter.


So she went to Urim. She entered the E-mud-kura at Urim, when she had entered the E-kic-nu-jal, the house of Nanna. She lamented before Nanna: 


" Father Nanna, don't let your daughter be killed in the underworld.
“ Don't let your precious metal be alloyed there with the dirt of the underworld.
“ Don't let your precious lapis lazuli be split there with the mason's stone.
“ Don't let your boxwood be chopped up there with the carpenter's wood.
“ Don't         let young lady Inana be killed in the underworld."


In his rage, Father Nanna answered Nincubura: 


" My daughter craved the great heaven and she craved the great below as well. 
“ The divine powers of the underworld are divine powers which should not be craved,
“ For whoever gets them must remain in the underworld.
“ Who, having got to that place, could then expect to come up again?"


Thus, Father Nanna did not help her in this matter. 


So she went to Eridug. In Eridug, when she entered the house of Enki. She lamented before Enki: 


" Father Enki, don't let anyone kill your daughter in the underworld.
“ Don't let your precious metal be alloyed there with the dirt of the underworld.
“ Don't let your precious lapis lazuli be split there with the mason's stone.
“ Don't let your boxwood be chopped up there with the carpenter's wood.
“ Don't let young lady Inana be killed in the underworld."


Father Enki answered Nincubura:


" What has my daughter done?
“ She has me worried.
“ What has the mistress of all the lands, the hierodule of An done?" 


He removed some dirt from the tip of his fingernail and created the kur-jara. He removed some dirt from the tip of his other fingernail and created the gala-tura. To the kur-jara he gave the life-giving plant. To the gala-tura he gave the life-giving water.


Then Father Enki spoke out to the gala-tura and the kur-jara:


" One of you sprinkle the life-giving plant over her, and the other the life-giving water.


“ Go, and direct your steps to the underworld.
“ Flit past the door like flies.
“ Slip through the door pivots like phantoms.
“ The mother who gave birth, 
“ Erec-ki-gala, on account of her children, is lying there.
“ Her holy shoulders are not covered by a linen cloth
“ Her breasts are not full like a cagan vessel.
“ Her nails are like a pickaxe upon her.
“ The hair on her head is bunched up as if it were leeks.
" When she says


“” Oh my heart”",


“ You are to say 


“” You are troubled, our mistress, oh your heart"”.


“ When she says 


“" Oh my liver”", 


“ You are to say 


“” You are troubled, our mistress, oh your liver”".


“ She will then ask


“" Who are you?
“” Speaking to you from my heart to your heart,
“” From my liver to your liver 
“” If you are gods, let me talk with you;
“” If you are mortals, may a destiny be decreed for you."” 


“ Make her swear this by heaven and earth.
“ Her servant will come
" They will offer you a riverful of water


“ Don't accept it.


“ They will offer you a field with its grain


“ Don't accept it, but say to her
” Give us the corpse hanging on the hook


“ She will answer


“" That is the corpse of your queen.”" 


“ Say to her


“ Whether it is that of our king, whether it is that of our queen,
“ Give it to us." 


“ She will give you the corpse hanging on the hook.
“ One of you sprinkle on it the life-giving plant and the other the life-giving water.
“ Thus, let Inana arise."


The gala-tura and the kur-jara paid attention to the instructions of Enki. They flitted through the door like flies. They slipped through the door pivots like phantoms. The mother who gave birth, Erec-ki-gala, because of her children, was lying there. 


Her holy shoulders were not covered by a linen cloth. Her breasts were not full like a cagan vessel. Her nails were like a pickaxe upon her. The hair on her head was bunched up as if it were leeks.


When she said: 


" Oh my heart", 


They said to her: 


" You are troubled, our mistress, oh your heart". 


When she said: 


" Oh my liver", 


They said to her: 


" You are troubled, our mistress, oh your liver". 


Then, she asked:


" Who are you? 
“ I tell you from my heart to your heart, from my liver to your liver
“ If you are gods, I will talk with you;
“ If you are mortals, may a destiny be decreed for you." 


They made her swear this by heaven and earth.


They were offered a river with its water. They did not accept it. They were offered a field with its grain, but they did not accept it. They said to her:


" Give us the corpse hanging on the hook." 


Holy Erec-ki-gala answered the gala-tura and the kur-jara: 


" The corpse is that of your queen." 


They said to her: 


" Whether it is that of our king or that of our queen, give it to us." 


They were given the corpse hanging on the hook. One of them sprinkled on it the life-giving plant and the other the life-giving water. And thus Inana arose.


Erec-ki-gala said to the gala-tura and the kur-jara: 


" Bring your queen Inana, your divine power has been seized." 


Inana, because of Enki's instructions, was about to ascend from the underworld. But as Inana was about to ascend from the underworld, the Anuna seized her: 


" Who has ever ascended from the underworld,
“ Who has ascended unscathed from the underworld?


“ If Inana is to ascend from the underworld,
“ Let her provide a substitute for herself."
Tablet IIII: 
So when Inana left the underworld, the one in front of her, though not a minister, held a sceptre in his hand; the one behind her, though not an escort, carried a mace at his hip, while the small demons, like a reed enclosure, and the big demons, like the reeds of a fence, restrained her on all sides.


Those who accompanied her knew no food. They knew no drink. They ate no flour offering, and drank no libation. They accept no pleasant gifts. They never enjoy the pleasures of the marital embrace, nor have any sweet children to kiss. 


They tear away the wife from a man's embrace. They snatch the son from a man's knee. They make the bride leave the house of her father-in-law. They take the child from the breasts of the wet-nurse. They crush no bitter garlic. They eat no fish, nor leeks. It was they, who accompanied Inana.


Inana ascended from the underworld, and Nincubura threw herself at her feet, still at the door of the Ganzer. She had sat in the dust and clothed herself in a filthy garment. The demons said to Holy Inana:


" Inana, proceed to your city, 
“ We will take her back."


Holy Inana answered the demons: 


" This is my minister of fair words, my escort of trustworthy words.
“ She did not forget my instructions.
“ She did not neglect the orders I gave her.


“ She made a lament for me on the ruin mounds.
“ She beat the drum for me in the sanctuaries.
“ She made the rounds of the gods' houses for me.


“ She lacerated her eyes for me, lacerated her nose for me.
“ She lacerated her ears for me in public.
“ In private, she lacerated her buttocks for me
“ Like a pauper, she clothed herself in a single garment.”


" All alone she directed her steps to the E-kur, to the house of Enlil,
“ And to Urim, to the house of Nanna, and to Eridug, to the house of Enki.
“ She brought me back to life.
“ How could I turn her over to you?
“ Let us go on. Let us go on to the Sig-kur-caga in Umma."


She came to the Sig-kur-caga in Umma. Cara, in his own city, threw himself at her feet. He had sat in the dust and dressed himself in a filthy garment. The demons said to Holy Inana: 


" Inana, proceed to your city, 
“ We will take him back."


Holy Inana answered the demons:


" Cara is my singer, my manicurist and my hairdresser.
“ How could I turn him over to you?
“ Let us go on. Let us go on to the E-muc-kalama in Bad-tibira."


She came to the E-muc-kalama in Bad-tibira. Lulal, in his own city, threw himself at her feet. He had sat in the dust and clothed himself in a filthy garment. The demons said to Holy Inana:


" Inana, proceed to your city,
“ We will take him back."


Holy Inana answered the demons: 


" Outstanding Lulal follows me at my right and my left.
“ How could I turn him over to you?
“ Let us go on.
“ Let us go on to the great apple tree in the plain of Kulaba."


They followed her to the great apple tree in the plain of Kulaba. There was Dumuzid clothed in a magnificent garment and seated magnificently on a throne. The demons seized him there by his thighs. 


The seven of them poured the milk from his churns. The seven of them shook their heads like bulls. They would not let the shepherd play the pipe and flute before her.


She looked at him, with the look of death. She spoke to him, with the speech of anger. She shouted at him, and it was the shout of heavy guilt: 


" How much longer?
“ Take him away." 


Holy Inana gave Dumuzid the shepherd into their hands.


Those who had accompanied her, who had come for Dumuzid, know no food. They knew no drink. They ate no flour offering and drank no libation. They accept no pleasant gifts. They never enjoy the pleasures of the marital embrace, nor have any sweet children to kiss. They tear away the wife from a man's embrace. They snatch the son from a man's knee. They make the bride leave the house of her father-in-law. 


Dumuzid let out a wail and turned very pale. The lad raised his hands toward heaven, to Utu: 


" Utu, you are my brother-in-law.
“ I am your relation by marriage.
“ I brought butter to your mother's house, I brought milk to Ningal's house.
“ Turn my hands into snake's hands and turn my feet into snake's feet,
“ So I can escape my demons, let them not keep hold of me."


Utu accepted his tears. Dumuzid's demons could not keep hold of him. Utu turned Dumuzid's hands into snake's hands. He turned his feet into snake's feet. Dumuzid escaped his demons.

        Holy Inana held her hand to her heart.


Holy Inana wept bitterly for her husband.


She tore at her hair like esparto grass:


" You wives who lie in your men's embrace,
“ Where is my precious husband?
“ You children who lie in your men's embrace,
“ Where is my precious child?
“ Where is my man?
“ Where is he? “


A fly spoke to holy Inanna: 


" If I show you where your man is,
“ What will be my reward?"


Holy Inana answered the fly:


" If you show me where my man is, I will give you this gift,


“ I will cover you with the splendour of heaven"


The fly helped Holy Inana. The young Lady Inana decreed the destiny of the fly: 


" In the beer-house and the tavern, may there be food and drink for you.
“ You will feast like the sons of the wise." 


Now Inana decreed this fate and thus it came to be.


Geshtinanna was weeping. Inana came up to the sister and took her by the hand:


" Now, alas, my dear husband, you for half the year and your sister for half the year:
“ When you are demanded, on that day you will stay,
“ When your sister is demanded, on that day you will be released." 


Thus Holy Inana gave Dumuzid as a substitute, his sister Jectin-ana.


Inanna and Enki:
Tablet I:
The Daughter of the Desert, Lady of the Steppe, put the šu-gura, the desert crown, on her head. Inana, when she went out to the shepherd, to the sheepfold, her genitals were remarkable.
 
When Inana descended from the heavens; her genitals were remarkable. She praised herself, full of delight at her genitals, she praised herself, full of delight at her genitals. She looked at them, she looked at her genitals:


" When I have gratified the lord An,
“ When I have made love, and he has become brilliant, 
“ When I have made love beautiful,
“ When I have made him glorious, and I have pleased him,
“ When I have made love perfect, luxuriant, and exuberant,
“ When I have made love, and heaven is shining,
“ When I have made my return, and I have made his face brilliant,
“ When I have made him shimmering,
“ I shall direct my steps to the Abzu,
“ To Eridug, I shall direct my steps to Enki,
“ In the Abzu, I myself, shall speak coaxingly to him, in the Abzu, 
“ In Eridug, I myself shall speak coaxingly to Enki."


To heaven she went. An’s favourite, went. The heavens shone. He kissed her there, and took delight in her pleasure. 


Seven days passed. 


Inana directed her steps to Abzu, to Eridug, saying:


" I, Inana, personally intend to go to the Abzu 
" I, Inana, personally intend to go to Eridug 
“ I shall utter a plea to Lord Enki. 
“ Like the sweet oil of the cedar, who will fall for my holy fragrant perfume?
“ It shall never escape me that I have been neglected by him who has had sex"


On that day the maiden, Inana, Holy Inana, directed her steps all by herself towards Enki's Abzu in Eridug. On that day, he of exceptional knowledge, who knows the divine powers in heaven and earth, who from his own dwelling already knows the intentions of the gods, Enki, the king of the Abzu, who, even before holy Inana had approached within six miles of the Abzu temple in Eridug, knew all about her enterprise.  


Enki spoke to his man, gave him instructions: 


" Come here, my man, listen to my words.
“ Lend me your ear, pay heed to what I say
" She comes, she will drink, she will eat. 


“ Come here!
“  I will welcome her here, so this you will do. 
“ The maiden Inana enters the Abzu, Eridug, 


” When the maiden Inana has entered the Abzu,Eridug,
“ Offer her butter cake to eat.
“ Let her be served cool refreshing water. Pour beer for her, in front of the Lions' Gate,
“ Make her feel as if she is in her girlfriend's house,
“ Make her feel welcome as a colleague.
“ You are to welcome Holy Inana at the holy table, at the table of An"


After Enki had spoken thus to him, his man, Isimud the minister followed his master's instructions closely. He let the maiden into the Abzu of Eridug; he let Inana into the Abzu of Eridug. 


When the maiden had entered, she got butter cake to eat. They poured cool refreshing water for her, and they gave her beer to drink, in front of the Lions' Gate. He made her feel as if she was in her girlfriend's house, and made her welcome as a colleague. He welcomed Holy Inana at the holy table, at the table of An.


So it came about that Enki and Inana were drinking beer together in the Abzu, and enjoying the taste of sweet wine. The bronze aga-vessels were filled to the brim, and the two of them started a competition, drinking from the bronze vessels of Uraš.


They drank until they were satiated and then drank more. One cup became two, two cups became four, four cups became eight. They were joyous and light-headed. 


Tablet II:


Enki spoke joyously:


" I will give them to Holy Inana, my daughter; may she not be denied!" 


Holy Inana received heroism, power, wickedness, righteousness, the plundering of cities, making lamentations, rejoicing. 


" In the name of my power, in the name of my Abzu,
“ I will give them to Holy Inana, my daughter; may she not be denied."


Holy Inana received deceit, the rebel lands, kindness, being on the move, being sedentary. 


" In the name of my power, in the name of my Abzu,
“ I will give them to Holy Inana, my daughter; may she not be denied."


Holy Inana received the craft of the carpenter, the craft of the coppersmith, the craft of the scribe, the craft of the smith, the craft of the leather-worker, the craft of the fuller, the craft of the builder, the craft of the reed-worker. 


" In the name of my power, in the name of my Abzu,
“ I will give them to Holy Inana, my daughter; may she not be denied."




Holy Inana received wisdom, attentiveness, holy purification rites, the shepherd's hut, piling up glowing charcoals, the sheepfold, respect, awe, reverent silence. 


" In the name of my power, in the name of my Abzu,
“ I will give them to Holy Inana, my daughter; may she not be denied."


Holy Inana received the bitter-toothed stone, the kindling of fire, the extinguishing of fire, hard work, fulfillment, the assembled family, descendants. 


" In the name of my power, in the name of my Abzu,
“ I will give them to Holy Inana, my daughter; may she not be denied."


Holy Inana received strife, triumph, counselling, comforting, judging, decision-making. 


" In the name of my power, in the name of my Abzu,
“ I will give them to Holy Inana, my daughter; may she be denied."


Holy Inana received all the divine powers Enki held.
 
" In the name of my power, in the name of my Abzu,
“ I will give them to Holy Inana, my daughter; may she not be denied."




Tablet III:


Inana, the holy woman, went, saying:


" He has given me righteousness. 
“ He has given me the plundering of cities, and the making of lamentations;
“ He has given me rejoicing.
" He has given me deceit, the rebel lands, and he has given me kindness.


“ He has given me, being on the move and he has given me, being sedentary.
" He has given me the craft of the carpenter, the craft of the coppersmith,
“ And he has given me the craft of the scribe.


“ He has given me the craft of the smith, and the craft of the leather-worker.
“ He has given me the craft of the fuller.
“ He has given me the craft of the builder and that of the reed-worker.
" He has given me wisdom and he has given me attentiveness.
“ He has given me the holy purification rites.


“ He has given me the shepherd's hut, and the sheepfold.
“ He has given me the piling up of glowing charcoals,
“ He has given me respect and awe; he has given me reverent silence


" He has given me the bitter-toothed firestarter, and the kindling of fire.
“ He has given me the extinguishing of fire.
“ He has given me hard work.


“ He has given me fulfillment, as he has given me the assembled family,
“ He has given me descendants,
“ He has given me strife, and he has given me triumph.
“ He has given me counselling.


“ He has given me all that he possesses!”


Tablet IIII:


Enki spoke to the minister Isimud: 


" Isimud, my minister, my Sweet Name of Heaven!" 


" Enki, my master, I am at your service. What is your wish?" 


Enki made ready to speak:


" Since she said that she would not yet depart from here for Unug-Kulaba,
“ That she would not yet depart from here, to the place where Utu is king,
“ Can I still reach her?" 


But Holy Inana had gathered up the divine powers and embarked onto the Boat of Heaven. The Boat of Heaven had already left the quay, as the effects of the beer cleared from him who had drunk beer. As the effects of the beer cleared from Father Enki, the Great Lord Enki turned his attention to the temple building. The lord looked up at the Abzu. King Enki turned his attention to Eridug.


Enki spoke to Isimud the minister:


" Isimud, my minister, my Sweet Name of Heaven!" 


" Enki, my master, I am at your service. What is your wish?" 


" Where are the office of en priest, the office of lagar priest, divinity,
“ The great and good crown, the royal throne?" 


Isimud gave reply:


" My master has given them to his daughter."


Enki spoke, again, to Isimud the minister: 


" Where are the noble sceptre, the staff and crook, the noble dress,
“ Shepherdship, kingship?" 


" My master has given them to his daughter."


" Where are the office of egir-zid priestess, the office of nin-diĝir priestess,
“ The office of išib priest, the office of lu-maḫ priest, the office of gudug priest?"
 " Where are constancy, going down to the underworld, coming up from the underworld, 
“ And the kur-ĝara priest?


" Where are the sword and club, the cultic functionary saĝ-ursaĝ, the black garment,
“ The colourful garment, the unkempt hair-style, the kempt hair-style?" 
" Where are my holy rites?" 


" Where are the standard, the quiver, sexual intercourse, kissing,
“ Prostitution, and brothel running?" 


" Where are forthright speech, deceitful speech, grandiloquent speech, boastful speech,
“ The cultic prostitute, the holy tavern?" 


" Where are the holy niĝin-ĝar shrine, the honour of the mistress of heaven,
“ Loud musical instruments, the art of song, venerable old age?"


" My master has given them to his daughter;
“ To her you have given all these things.”


Tablet V:




The prince spoke to his minister Isimud, Enki addressed the Sweet Name of Heaven: 


" Isimud, my minister, my Sweet Name of Heaven!" 


" Enki, my master, I am at your service. What is your wish?" 


" Where has the Boat of Heaven reached now?" 


"It has just now reached the Urim quay." 


" Go now!
“ The Enkum, the Executive Lords, 
“ They are to take the Boat of Heaven away from her!"


He went to the maiden. The minister Isimud spoke to Holy Inana: 


" My lady! Your father has sent me, Inana, your father has sent me to you.
“ What your father said was very serious; what Enki spoke was very serious.
“ His important words cannot be countermanded." 


Holy Inana replied to him: 


" What has my father said to you, what has he spoken?
“ Why should his important words not be countermanded?" 


" My master has spoken to me, Enki has said to me: 


“" Inana may travel to Unug,
“” But you are to get the Boat of Heaven back to Eridug for me.""


Holy Inana spoke to the minister Isimud: 


" How could my father have changed what he said to me?
“ How could he have altered his promise as far as I am concerned?
“ How could he have discredited his important words to me?


“ Was it falsehood that my father said to me, did he speak falsely to me?
“ Has he sworn falsely by the name of his power and by the name of his Abzu?
“ Has he duplicitously sent you to me as a messenger?" 


Now as these words were still in her mouth, he got the Enkum to seize hold of the Boat of Heaven. Holy Inana addressed her minister Ninšubur: 


" Come, my good minister of E-ana,
“ My fair-spoken minister, my envoy of reliable words.
“ Water has never touched your hand, water has never touched your feet!"


So Inana got hold again of the divine powers which had been presented to her, and the Boat of Heaven; and, for the second time the prince spoke to his minister, Isimud. Enki addressed the Sweet Name of Heaven: 


" Isimud, my minister, my Sweet Name of Heaven!" 


" Enki, my master, I am at your service. What is your wish?" 


" Where has the Boat of Heaven reached now?" 


" It has just now reached the holy E-ana" 


" Go now! The fifty giants of Eridug are to take the Boat of Heaven away from her."


The minister Isimud spoke to holy Inana: 


" My lady! Your father has sent me, Inana, your father has sent me to you.
“ What your father said was very serious; what Enki spoke was very serious.
“ His important words cannot be countermanded." 


Holy Inana replied to him: 


" What has my father said to you, what has he spoken?
“ Why should his important words not be countermanded?" 


" My master has spoken to me, Enki has said to me: 


“" Inana may travel to Unug, 
“” But you are to get the Boat of Heaven back to Eridug for me.""


Holy Inana spoke to the minister, Isimud: 


" How could my father have changed what he said to me?
“ How could he have altered his promise as far as I am concerned?
“ How could he have discredited his important words to me?


“ Was it falsehood that my father said to me, did he speak falsely to me? 
“ Has he sworn falsely by the name of his power and by the name of his Abzu?
“ Has he duplicitously sent you to me as a messenger?" 


Now as these words were still in her mouth, he got the fifty giants of Eridug to seize hold of the Boat of Heaven. Holy Inana addressed her minister Ninšubur: 


" Come, my good minister of E-ana,
“ My fair-spoken minister, my envoy of reliable words.
“ Water has never touched your hand, water has never touched your feet!"


Inana got hold again of the divine powers which had been presented to her, and the Boat of Heaven; and, for the third time the prince spoke to his minister, Isimud. Enki addressed the Sweet Name of Heaven: 


" Isimud, my minister, my Sweet Name of Heaven!" 


" Enki, my master, I am at your service. What is your wish?" 


" Where has the Boat of Heaven reached now?" 


" It has just now reached the UL.MA hill." 


" Go now! The fifty laḫama of the subterranean waters
“ They are to take the Boat of Heaven away from her!"


The minister Isimud spoke to Holy Inana: 


" My lady! Your father has sent me, Inana, your father has sent me to you.
“ What your father said was very serious; what Enki spoke was very serious.

Now as these words were still in her mouth, he got The fifty laḫama of the subterranean waters to seize hold of the Boat of Heaven. Holy Inana addressed her minister Ninšubur: 


" Come, my good minister of E-ana,

So Inana got hold again of the divine powers which had been presented to her, and the Boat of Heaven; and, for the fourth time the prince spoke to his minister Isimud. Enki addressed the Sweet Name of Heaven: 


" Isimud, my minister, my Sweet Name of Heaven!" 


" Enki, my master, I am at your service! What is your wish?" 


" Where has the Boat of Heaven reached now?" 


" It has just now reached the Field Hill." 


" Go now! All the great fish, together, 
“ They are to take the Boat of Heaven away from her!"


The minister Isimud spoke to Holy Inana: 


" My lady! Your father has sent me, Inana, your father has sent me to you.
“ What your father said was very serious, what Enki spoke was very serious.
“ His important words cannot be countermanded." 


Holy Inana replied to him: 


" What has my father said to you, now, what has he spoken? 
“ Why should his important words not be countermanded?" 


" My master has spoken to me, Enki has said to me: 


“" Inana may travel to Unug,
“” But you are to get the Boat of Heaven back to Eridug for me.”"


Holy Inana spoke to the minister, Isimud: 


" How could my father have changed what he said to me? 
“ How could he have altered his promise as far as I am concerned?
“ How could he have discredited his important words to me?


“ Was it falsehood that my father said to me, did he speak falsely to me?
“ Has he sworn falsely by the name of his power and by the name of his Abzu?
“ Has he duplicitously sent you to me as a messenger?"


Now as these words were still in her mouth, he got all the great fish, together, to seize hold of the Boat of Heaven. Holy Inana addressed her minister Ninšubur: 


" Come, my good minister of E-ana,
“ My fair-spoken minister, my envoy of reliable words.
“ Water has never touched your hand, water has never touched your feet!"


So Inana got hold again of the divine powers which had been presented to her, and the Boat of Heaven; and, for the fifth time the prince spoke to his minister, Isimud. Enki addressed the Sweet Name of Heaven: 


" Isimud, my minister, my Sweet Name of Heaven!" 


"Enki, my master, I am at your service. What is your wish?" 


"Where has the Boat of Heaven reached now?" 


"It has just now reached E-negir" 


"Go now! The Muande-e, the guardians of Unug,
“ They are to take the Boat of Heaven away from her!"


The minister Isimud spoke to Holy Inana: 


" My lady! Your father has sent me, Inana, your father has sent me to you.
“ What your father said was very serious, what Enki spoke was very serious.
“ His important words cannot be countermanded." 


Holy Inana replied to him: 


" What has my father said to you, what has he spoken?
“ Why should his important words not be countermanded?" 


" My master has spoken to me, Enki has said to me: 


“" Inana may travel to Unug,
“” But you are to get the Boat of Heaven back to Eridug for me.""


Holy Inana spoke to the minister Isimud: 


" How could my father have changed what he said to me?
“ How could he have altered his promise as far as I am concerned?
“ How could he have discredited his important words to me?


“ Was it falsehood that my father said to me, did he speak falsely to me?
“ Has he sworn falsely by the name of his power and by the name of his Abzu?
“ Has he duplicitously sent you to me as a messenger?" 


Now as these words were still in her mouth, he got the Muande-e, the guardians of Unug, to seize hold of the Boat of Heaven. Holy Inana addressed her minister Ninšubur:


" Come, my good minister of E-ana,
“ My fair-spoken minister, my envoy of reliable words.
“ Water has never touched your hand, water has never touched your feet!"




So Inana got hold, again, of the divine powers which had been presented to her, and the Boat of Heaven; and, for the sixth time the prince spoke to his minister, Isimud. Enki addressed the Sweet Name of Heaven: 


" Isimud, my minister, my Sweet Name of Heaven!" 


" Enki, my master, I am at your service. What is your wish?" 


" Where has the Boat of Heaven reached now?" 


" It has just now reached the Surungal canal , to the Id-surungal she has arrived." 


" Go now!
“ The Surungal canal inspectors are to take the Boat of Heaven away from her!
“ Take the Boat of Heaven away from Holy Inana."


The minister Isimud spoke to Holy Inana: 


" My lady! Your father has sent me, Inana, your father has sent me to you.
“ What your father said was very serious, what Enki spoke was very serious.
“ His important words cannot be countermanded."


Holy Inana replied to him: 


" What has my father said to you, what has he spoken?
“ Why should his important words not be countermanded?" 


" My master has spoken to me, Enki has said to me: 


“" Inana may travel to Unug,
“” But you are to get the Boat of Heaven back to Eridug for me.""


Holy Inana spoke to the minister Isimud: 


" How could my father have changed what he said to me?
“ How could he have altered his promise as far as I am concerned?
“ How could he have discredited his important words to me?


“ Was it falsehood that my father said to me, did he speak falsely to me?
“ Has he sworn falsely by the name of his power and by the name of his Abzu?
“ Has he duplicitously sent you to me as a messenger?"


Now as these words were still in her mouth, he got the Surungal canal to seize hold of the Boat of Heaven from Inana. Holy Inana addressed her minister Ninšubur: 


" Come, my good minister of E-ana,
“ My fair-spoken minister, my envoy of reliable words.
“ Water has never touched your hand, water has never touched your feet!"




So Inana got hold, again, of the divine powers which had been presented to her, and the Boat of Heaven; then, to Unug-Kubala she arrived on the Boat of Heaven. Ninšubur was with her on the Boat of Heaven. 


A seventh time the Prince spoke to his minister, Isimud. Enki addressed the Sweet Name of Heaven: 


" Isimud, my minister, my Sweet Name of Heaven!" 


" Enki, my master, I am at your service. What is your wish?" 


" Where has the Boat of Heaven reached now?" 


" It has now reached Unug-Kubala, she has finally arrived." 


The great princely scion, Holy Enki, went. Holy Inana arrived on the Boat of Heaven. Holy Inana at that time was in Unug


Her minister Ninšubur spoke to Holy Inana: 


" My lady, today you have brought the Boat of Heaven to the Gate of Joy,
“ To Unug Kulaba; 


“ Now, there will be rejoicing in our city,
“ The barges on our river shall be full and rejoice"


Holy Inana replied to her: 


" Today I have brought the Boat of Heaven to the Gate of Joy,
“ To Unug Kulaba;


“ It shall pass along the street magnificently.
“ The people shall stand in the street full of awe."


The people came in joy. The old men of the city were given comfort in Unug. The old women were in counsel, the young men carried strength of arms, the children cried out in joy in Unug.


Inana spoke aloud:


" The king shall hold a festival for the Boat of Heaven.
“ He shall recite great prayers.
“ The king shall slaughter bulls, shall sacrifice sheep.
“ He shall pour beer from a bowl.


“ He shall have the šem and ala drums sound,
“ And have the sweet-sounding tigi instruments play.
“ The foreign lands shall declare my greatness. My people shall utter my praise."


When she had spoken thus, the Boat of Heaven, at the Gate of Joy at Unug-Kulaba, passed magnificently along the street. It reached the maiden's house, and she set its place. She drew water from the purified well, her principal well, with the divine powers which had been presented to her with the Boat of Heaven, at the Ĝipar Gate. To the Agrun Chamber she went, and Holy Inana washed the Boat of Heaven clean.


The Prince addressed his minister Isimud, Enki spoke to the Sweet Name of Heaven: 


" Isimud, my minister, my Sweet Name of Heaven!" 


"Enki, my master, I am at your service. What is your wish?" 


" Where has the Boat of Heaven reached now?" 


" It has just now reached the White Quay." 


" Go now, with admiration. Go with admiration to the Boat of Heaven. 
“  You shall present Holy Inana with proper admiration, 


“ Go now."




Tablet VI:
The minister, Isimud, approached holy Inana in admiration saying:


" Inana, you have brought with you the office of en priest,
“ You have brought with you, the office of lagar priest,
“ You have brought with you, divinity,


“ You have brought with you, the great and good crown,
“ You have brought with you, the royal throne.
" You have brought with you, the noble sceptre,
“ You have brought with you, the staff and crook,
“ You have brought with you, the noble dress,
“ You have brought with you, shepherdship,
“ And you have brought with you, kingship.


" You have brought with you, the office of egir-zid priestess,
“ You have brought with you, the office of nin-diĝir priestess,
“ You have brought with you, the office of išib priest,
“ You have brought with you, the office of lu-maḫ priest,
“ And you have brought with you, the office of gudug priest.


" You have brought with you, constancy,
“ You have brought with you, going down to the underworld,
“ You have brought with you, coming up from the underworld,


“ You have brought with you, the kur-ĝara priest
“ You have brought with you, the cultic functionary, saĝ-ursaĝ,

“ You have brought with you, the colourful garment,
“ You have brought with you, the kempt hair-style, and the unkempt hair-style


" You have brought with you, sword and club,
" You have brought with you, the standard, 
“ And you have brought with you, the quiver,


“ You have brought with you, sexual intercourse,
“ You have brought with you, kissing,
“ You have brought with you, prostitution, 
“ And you have brought with you, house running.

“ You have brought with you, deceitful speech,
“ You have brought with you, grandiloquent speech,
“ And you have brought with you, boastful speech,

" You have brought with you, heroism, 
“ You have brought with you, power,


“ You have brought with you, wickedness,
“ You have brought with you, righteousness,
“ You have brought with you, the plundering of cities,
“ You have brought with you, making lamentations,
“ And you have brought with you, rejoicing.


" You have brought with you, deceit,
“ You have brought with you, the rebel lands,
“ You have brought with you, kindness, 
“ You have brought with you, being on the move,
“ And you have brought with you, being sedentary.


" You have brought with you, the craft of the carpenter,
“ You have brought with you, the craft of the coppersmith,
“ You have brought with you, the craft of the scribe, 
“ You have brought with you, the craft of the smith,
“ You have brought with you, the craft of the leather-worker,
“ You have brought with you, the craft of the fuller,
“ You have brought with you, the craft of the builder,
“ You have brought with you, the craft of the reed-worker.


" You have brought with you, wisdom, 
“ You have brought with you, attentiveness,
“ You have brought with you, holy purification rites,
“ You have brought with you, the shepherd's hut,
“ You have brought with you, piling up glowing charcoals,
“ You have brought with you, the sheepfold, 


“ You have brought with you, respect,
“ You have brought with you, awe, 
“ You have brought with you, reverent silence


" You have brought with you, the bitter-toothed stone,
“ You have brought with you, the kindling of fire,
“ You have brought with you, the extinguishing of fire,
“ You have brought with you, hard work,
“ And you have brought with you, fulfillment,


“ You have brought with you the, assembled family,
“ You have brought with you, descendants.
" You have brought with you, strife,
“ And triumph,


“ You have brought with you, counselling,
“ You have brought with you, comforting,
“ You have brought with you, judging,
“ You have brought with you, decision-making.
" You have brought with you, the establishing of plans,
“ And the attractiveness of women,


“ You have brought with you, strength to handle the perfect divine powers,
“ You have brought with you, small powers, 
“ You have brought with you, the exalted powers,
“ You have brought with you, the holy tigi, holy lilis, ub, meze and ala-drums,


“ You have brought with you, the rites of Holy An,
“ You have brought with you, the staff of Holy An,
“ You have brought with you, the sceptre of Holy An,
“ You have brought with you, the dias of Holy An,
“ You have brought with you, the diadem of Holy An,
“ You have brought with you, the word of Holy An,
“ You have brought with you, all of the wine and beer."


Where the woman went, joy followed. She named it, with the name, the House Ganzer is Rebuilt. 


Where the trader said: 


" Fifty shekels", but when he brought it there was less, she named that place with the name Potsherds and Scrap Metal. 


Where the boat came to rest, she named it, with the name, the Good Quay. 


Where the boat came to dock at the quay, she named that place, with the name, White Quay. 


Where the boat unloaded its goods, she named that place, with the name, Blue Quay.


Enki spoke to holy Inana: 


" In the name of my power, in the name of my Abzu, 
“ I will establish a shrine for you in my Abzu,
“ For you, the woman."


Inana spoke: 


" Why has this one now entered here?
“ And why is he taking the divine powers from me?"


When she had finished her words, Isimud then spoke: 


" May the gods rejoice in your name,"
" May there be daily, a festival, 
“ May the people pass their time, here, at the gate of your Ĝipar! 


“ May the citizens of your city, Inana, the citizens of Unug, live in abundance!
“ And as for you, Enki may your city, Eridug prosper, 
“ As I have indeed restored the divine powers"




Chapter 5
Ziusudra:
Tablet I: Shaping of Clay:


When the gods instead of man did the work, bore the loads, the gods' load was too great. The work, too hard; the trouble, too much. 


The great Anunnaki made the Igigi carry the workload sevenfold.


Anu, their father was king. Their counsellor, warrior Enlil. Their chamberlain was Ninurta; their canal-controller, Enuggi. They took the box of lots, and cast them. The gods made the division. 


Anu went up to the sky. Enlil took the earth for his people. The bolt which bars the sea was assigned to far-sighted Enki.


When Anu had gone up to the sky, and the gods of the Abzu had gone below, the Anunnaki of the sky made the Igigi bear the workload. The gods had to dig out canals, they had to clear channels, the lifelines of the land. 


The gods dug out the Tigris river bed and then dug out the Euphrates. In the deep they set up the Abzu of the land. They toiled inside it. Then, raised its top, the glory of all the mountains. 


They were counting the years of loads. They established the great marsh. For 3600 years they bore the excess, hard work night and day. 


They groaned, blaming each other. They grumbled over the masses of excavated soil: 


“ Let us confront our lord, the Chamberlain,
“ Let him relieve us of our hard work! 
“ Come, let us carry the lord, the Counsellor of Gods, from his dwelling.
“ Let us carry Enlil, the warrior, from his dwelling.”


Gibil, then made his voice heard and spoke to the gods his brothers:


“ Come! Let us carry the Counsellor of the Gods, the warrior, from his dwelling. 
“ Come!


“ Now, cry battle!
“ Let us mix fight with battle!' 


The gods listened to his speech, and set fire to their tools. They put aside their spades for fire. They put aside their loads for the fire god. 


They flared up.


When they reached the gate of the warrior Enlil's dwelling, it was the night. The house was surrounded. The god had not yet realized. 


It was the middle watch. 


The E-Kur was surrounded. 


Enlil had not realized. 


Yet, Kalkal, Enlil's gate master, was attentive. 


He had the gate closed. He held the lock closed and watched the gate, vigilantly. Kalkal roused Nusku. They both listened to the noise of the Igigi. Nusku, then, roused his master, and made him get out of bed: 


“ My lord, your house is surrounded,
“ A rabble is running around your door!


“ Enlil!
“ It is your house they surround,
“ The Igigi are running around your door!”


Enlil had weapons brought to his dwelling. Enlil made his voice heard. He spoke to the vizier, Nusku: 


“ Nusku, bar your door,
“ Take up your weapons;
“ Stand firm in front of me.' 


Nusku barred his door. He took up his weapons and stood in front of Enlil. Nusku made his voice heard and spoke to the warrior Enlil: 


“ O’ my Lord Enlil, 
“ Your face is sallow as tamarisk!
“ Why do you fear your own sons?
“ Send for An to be brought down to you,
“ Have Enki fetched into your presence.” 


So, he sent for An to be brought down to him; Enki was fetched into his presence. 


An, King of the Sky, was present. Enki, King of the Abzu, attended. The Great Anunnaki were all present. Enlil got up and the case was put forward. Enlil made his voice heard and spoke to the Great Gods:


“ Is it against me that they have risen?
“ Shall I do battle with them?


“ What did I see with my own eyes?
“ A rabble was running around my door!”


Anu made his voice heard and spoke to the warrior Enlil,


“ Let Nusku go out
“ Find out the word of the Igigi who have surrounded your door.”


Enlil made his voice heard and spoke to the vizier Nusku:


“ Nusku, open your door,
” Take up your weapons and stand before me!


“ In the assembly of all the gods, bow,
“ Then stand and tell them:


” Your Father An, your counsellor warrior Enlil,
” Your chamberlain Ninurta, and your canal-controller Enuggi have sent me to say,
” Who is in charge of this rabble? Who is in charge of this fighting?
” Who declared war? 
” Who ran to the door of Enlil?"' 


Nusku opened his door, took up his weapons. He went out before Enlil, in the assembly of all the gods he bowed. He stood and delivered Enlil's message. 


Gibil, leader of the Igigi revolt, made his voice heard and spoke to Nusku:


“ Every single one of us gods declared war!


“ We have put a stop to the digging. The load is too excessive, it is killing us.
“ Our work is too hard, the trouble is too much!
“ So, every single one of us gods has agreed to complain to Enlil.” 


Nusku took his weapons, and returned to Enlil. There Nusku delivered the answer of the Igigi. Enlil listened to his speech. His tears flowed. 


Enlil, then spoke guardedly. He addressed An:


“ Noble One, take a decree with you to the sky, 
“ Show your strength while the Anunnaki are sitting before you,
“ Call up one rebellious god and let them cast him for destruction.” 


An made his voice heard and spoke to the gods his sons:


“ What are we complaining of? 
“ Their work was indeed too hard, their trouble was too much.
“ Every day the earth resounded with their groans and cries.
“ The warning signal was loud enough,
“ We kept hearing the noise, but we ignored them.
“ They have every right to revolt and complain to the house of Enlil!”


Enki made his voice heard and spoke to the gods his brothers, 


“ Why are we blaming them?
“ Their work was too hard, their trouble was too much.
“ Every day the earth resounded with their groans and cries.
“ The warning signal was loud enough,
“ We kept hearing the noise, but we ignored them. 


“ There is one, Belet-ili the womb-goddess is present 
“ Let her create primeval man, let her create a mortal man 
“ So that he may bear the yoke, the work of Enlil,
“ Let man bear the load of the gods.”  


They called up the goddess, asked the midwife of the gods, wise Mami:


“ You are the womb-goddess to be the creator of mankind.
“ Create a mortal, so that he may bear the yoke, 
“ Let him bear the yoke, the work of Enlil!
“ Let man bear the load of the gods!” 


She gazed at Enki. Nintu made her voice heard and spoke to the great gods:


“ It is not proper for me to make him.
“ That work is Enki's.


“ He must make everything pure. 


“ If Enki gives me the clay, then I will do it.”


Enki made his voice heard and spoke to the Great Gods:


“ On the first, seventh, and fifteenth of the month ,
“ I, Enki, shall make a purification by washing.
“ Then, one god should be slaughtered and the gods can be purified by immersion.


“ Nintu shall mix clay with his flesh and blood,
“ Then a god and a man will be mixed together in clay.


“ Let us hear the drumbeat forever after,
“ Let a ghost come into existence from the gods flesh,
“ Let her proclaim it as his living sign,
“ And let the ghost exist so as not to forget the slain god’s flesh.”


The Great Anunnaki who assigns the fates, they answered: 


“ Yes“, in the assembly. 


On the first, seventh, and fifteenth of the month, Enki made a purification by washing. Geshtu-e, who had intelligence. They slaughtered him in their assembly. 


Nintu mixed clay with his flesh. Nintu mixed clay with his blood. They heard the drumbeat forever after. 


A ghost came into existence from the god’s flesh. And Nintu proclaimed it as his living sign. The ghost existed so as not to forget the slain god. 


After she had mixed that clay, she called up the Anunnaki, the Great Gods. She called upon the Igigi, the Great Gods. They spat spittle upon the clay.


Mami made her voice heard and spoke to the great gods:


“ I have carried out perfectly, the work that you ordered of me.
“ You have slaughtered a god, together with his intelligence.
“ I have relieved you of your hard work, and imposed your load upon man.


“ You have bestowed noise and woe upon mankind,
“ But I have undone the fetter and granted your freedom.”


They listened to this speech of hers, and they were freed from anxiety. They fell down and kissed her feet: 


“ We used to call you Mami but now your name shall be
“ Mistress of All Gods.”


Far-sighted Enki and wise Mami went into the Room of Fate. The womb-goddesses were assembled there. Enki trod the clay in her presence. Mami kept reciting an incantation; for Enki, staying in her presence, made her recite it. 


When she had finished her incantation, she pinched off fourteen pieces of clay. She set seven pieces on the right. She set seven on the left. Between them she put down a mud brick. 


She made use of a reed, and split it open, to cut the umbilical cords. She called up the wise and knowledgeable womb-goddesses, for the seven and seven; seven created males, seven created females, for the womb-goddess is the creator of fate. 


Enki paired them two by two.


He paired them two by two in her presence as Mami made these rules for people:


“ In the house of a woman who is giving birth, 
“ The mud brick shall be put down for seven days,
“ Belet-ili, wise Mami, shall be honoured.


“ The midwife shall rejoice in the house of the woman who gives birth,
“ And when the woman gives birth to the baby, 
“ The mother of the baby shall sever the cord herself. 


“ A man to a woman, he shall seed.
“ A girl shall be ready by the sign of her bosoms,
“ A young man, by the beard upon his cheek. 


“ In the gardens and the waysides a wife and her husband shall choose each other.”


The womb-goddesses were assembled where Nintu was present. They counted the months, and called up the tenth month as the term of fates. When the tenth month came, she slipped in her staff and opened the womb. 


Her face was glad and joyful. She covered her head, performed the midwifery, and put on her belt. She said a blessing. She made a drawing in flour, and put down a mud brick in the center of it: 


“ I myself have created it, my hands have made it. 
“ The midwife shall rejoice in the house of the qadishtu-priestess.
“  Wherever a woman gives birth and the baby's mother severs herself,
“ The mud brick shall be put down for nine days.


“ Nintu the womb-goddess shall be honoured. 
“ She shall call their goddess, Mami,
“ She shall honour the womb-goddess,


“ Lay down the linen cloth, when the bed is laid out in their house,
“ A wife and her husband shall choose each other. 
“ Ishtar shall rejoice in the wife-husband relationship,


“ In the father-in-law's house,
“ Celebration shall last for nine days, and they shall call Ishtar, Ishhara
“ On the fifteenth day, the fixed time of fate, 
“ She shall call upon the womb-goddess and Inanna for blessings upon the child.' 


Enlil was very pleased with the work of far-sighted Enki and wise Mami. The new mortals grew quickly and soon they took hold of tools, made new picks and spades; they made big, the canals so as to feed the people and sustain the gods. 


Tablet II: Pestilence:


600 years had passed. Less than 600 years had passed, and the country became too wide. 


The people became too numerous. The country was as noisy as a bellowing bull. The god grew restless at their racket. Enlil had to listen to their noise. 


He organized the assembly, and addressed the Great Gods:


“ Oppressive has become the clamour of mankind,
“ I am losing sleep because of their roar.


“ Let there be chills, let Namtar blow upon them like a storm,
“  Let there be aches, dizziness, chills and fever!”


Then, there developed chills. The pestilence promptly put an end to their clamour. Like a storm, it blew upon them. Aches, dizziness, chills, and fever seized the inhabitants in the land. 


Now there was one,  Ziusudra, endowed with wisdom. His mind was alert. He, whose ear was open to his god, Enki. He would speak with his god; his god would speak with him. Ziusudra made his voice heard and spoke to his lord:


“ How long will the gods make us suffer? 
“ Will they make us suffer illness forever?
“ Oh lord, the people cry out!


“ The anger of the gods consumes the land
“ Yet it is you who has created us,
“ Let there cease the sickness.”


Enki made his voice hard and spoke to his servant Ziusudra:


“ Call the elders, and the senior men!
“ Start an uprising in your own house,
“ Let heralds proclaim my command, 
“ Let them make a loud noise in the land: 


“ Do not revere your gods,
“ Do not pray to your goddesses,
“ But search out the door of Namtara.
“ Bring a baked loaf into his presence.
“ Make the flour offering reach him,


“ May he be shamed by the presents,
“ And wipe away his hand.”


Ziusudra took the order, gathered the elders to his door. Ziusudra made his voice heard and spoke to the elders:


“ I have called the elders and the senior men.
“ Start an uprising in your own house,
“ Let heralds proclaim my command, 
“ Let them make a loud noise in the land: 


“ Do not revere your gods,
“ Do not pray to your goddesses,
“ But search out the door of Namtara.
“ Bring a baked loaf into his presence.
“ May the flour offering reach him.
“ May he be shamed by the presents,
“ And wipe away his hand.”


The elders listened to his speech. They built a temple for Namtar in the city. The heralds proclaimed; they made a loud noise in the land. They did not revere their gods. They did not pray to their goddesses, but searched out the door of Namtar.


They brought a baked loaf into his presence. They made the flour offerings reach him. He was shamed by the presents, and Namtar wiped away his punishing hand. The suruppu-disease left them, and the gods went back to their regular offerings. 
Tablet II: Famine:


600 years had passed. Less than 600 years had passed, and the country became too wide. The people became numerous. The land bellowed like an oxen. 


The god grew restless at their clamour. Enlil had to listen to their noise; then, he addressed the Great Gods:


“ Abundance, do not arrange for them.
“ The people have not diminished,


“ Are they more numerous than before? 


“ The noise of mankind has become too much,
“ Because of their clamour, I am disturbed,
“ Because of their uproar, sleep cannot seize me.


“ Cut off food supplies to the peoples!
“ Let the fig tree be cut off,
“ Let the flour be cut off from the people!
“ Let the greens be too scant for their hunger,
“ Let Adad wipe away his rain!


“ Below, let the flood be dammed up.
“ Let it not rise from the source.
“ Let the wind go, let it strip the ground bare!
“ Let the clouds gather but drop no rain!


“ Let the land withdraw its yield,
“ Let the yield of the field be a diminished harvest,
“ Let it turn the breast of Nissaba.


“ During the nights let the fields turn white,
“ Let the broad plain bring forth potash,
“ Let the earth's womb revolt, that no plant come forth, no grain sprout. 
“ Let fever be placed upon the people, 
“ Let the womb be bound so that it issue not offspring,
“ No happiness shall come to them, let their pleas be dejected!”


They cut off the fig tree for the people. In their bellies the greens became too few. Above Adad made scarce his rain. Below was dammed up. The flood rose not from its source. 


The land withdrew its yield. Nisaba no longer offered her breast. During the nights the fields turned white. The broad plain brought forth potash. The earth's womb revolted, so that no plant came forth, nor grain sprouted. 


Upon the people fever was placed. The womb was bound so that it could not issue forth offspring. 


In the first year, they ate old grain. When the second year arrived, the stores were exhausted. When the third year arrived, the people became hostile in their homes.


When the fourth year arrived, they became cramped. Their wide places became too narrow. Downcast, people wandered in the streets. 


When the fifth year arrived, the daughter sought entry to the mother. The mother opened not, the door to her daughter. The daughter watched the balance of the mother. The mother watched the balance of the daughter.


When the sixth year arrived, they prepared the daughter for a meal. The child, they prepared as food. Only a few households remained, for one house devoured the other. 


The faces were covered with scabs, like malt. Like ghosts of the dead their faces were veiled. The people lived with bated breath. 


Now there was one, Ziusudra, endowed with wisdom. His mind was alert. He, whose ear was open to his god, Enki. He would speak with his god; his god would speak with him. He approached the gate of his god. 


Opposite the river, he places his bed. Ziusudra made his voice heard and spoke to his lord:


“ How long will the gods make us suffer? 
“ Will they make us suffer from drought forever?


“ Oh Lord, the people cry out!


“ The anger of the gods consumes the land,
“ Yet it is who has created us,
“ Let the springs flow forth.
“ Let Ishkur’s rains burst free
“ Let the abundant field return.”


Enki made his voice heard and spoke to his servant Ziusudra:


“ Call the elders, and the senior men!
“ Start an uprising in your own house,
“ Let heralds proclaim my command, 
“ Let them make a loud noise in the land: 


“ Do not revere your gods,
“ Do not pray to your goddesses,
“ But search out the house of Adad.
“ Bring an offering of gold and silver into his presence,
“ Make the blood of vine offering reach him.


“ May he be shamed by the presents
“ And wipe away his hand.”


Ziusudra took the order, gathered the elders to his door. Ziusudra made his voice heard and spoke to the elders:


“ I have called the elders and the senior men
“ Start an uprising in your own house,
“ Let heralds proclaim my command, 
“ Let them make a loud noise in the land: 


“ Do not revere your gods,
“ Do not pray to your goddesses,
“ But search out the house of Adad.
“ Bring an offering of gold and silver into his presence,
“ Make the blood of vine offering reach him.
“ May he be shamed by the presents,
“ And wipe away his hand."


The elders listened to his speech. They prepared an offering. They went to the temple of Adad, they went to his city. 


The heralds proclaimed his command, and made a loud noise in the land. They did not revere their gods. They did not pray to their goddesses, but searched out the door of Adad.


They brought gold and silver into his presence. They made the wine offerings reach him. He was shamed by the presents, and Adad cried for the people. The rains returned and abundance once again flowed from Nissaba’s bosom, and the gods went back to their regular offerings. 


600 years had passed. Less than 600 years had passed. 


Dream of The Turtle:


In ancient times, the hero lay down to dream. 


He woke up; it was a dream! 


He shivered; it was sleep! 


He rubbed his eyes.
