“ Unto the ridge of the loam of the earth.
“ Toward the two hills which block the way to the underworld.


“ Lift the mount on your hands, the elevation upon your palms,
“ Descend to the depths of the earth,
“ Be counted among those who go down into the earth.
“ Set your faces, be off on your way into his city, the pit, the mire
“ The low throne that he sits on,
“ Filth! The land of his inheritance.


“ Yet beware, divine messengers, approach not Divine Mot,
“ Lest he make you like a lamb in his mouth.
“ Lest ye be crushed like a kid in his gullet.
“ Even the Gods’ Torch Shapash, Goddess of the Sun,
“ She who wings over heaven’s expanse, is in Mot El’s Beloved’s hand!
“ The heavens are wearied by the hand of the Beloved of ‘El, Mot.
“ From a thousand fields, ten thousands acres, to Mot’s feet bow and fall down.
“ Prostrate you and show him honor.
“ Say unto Divine Mot, El's son, Death,
“ Declare unto El’s Darling, Ghazir, 
“ Message of Puissant Ba’al, word of the Mighty Warrior:


“ My house I have builded of silver, my palace, indeed, of gold,
“ Of the precious cedars of Lebanon, of most pure lapis lazuli,
“ I shall have no rival!”        


Away sped Gapn of the Vine, and Ugar of the Field, unto the pit of Mot; away from whom they get message.


Mot gave his reply to the messengers of Baal:


“ For all that you smote, if thou goest fighting Lotan, the Evasive Serpent,
“ If he is finished off;
“ If now thou wouldst try to destroy the wriggling, twisting, tortuous slant, the serpent,
“ The Tyrant, Shalyat of Seven Heads,
“ The heavens will burn up and droop helpless,
“ For I, myself, will crush you in pieces!


“ Indeed you must come down
“ You will surely descend into the throat of El's son, Divine Mot,
“ Death,
“ Into the watery depths, the gorge of the Beloved of El, the Youth Ghazir the Hero!"


The gods departed and tarried not.


Indeed they set their faces from the tomb of the Godly Mot, from the pit of El’s Beloved Ghazir. The gods twain departed, tarrying not, to Ba’al of the Summit Zaphon. Then Gapn and Ugar declared:


" Message of El's son, Divine Mot, Death,
“ Word of the Beloved of El, the Youth Ghazir the Hero:


““ Mine is the appetite of lions in the waste,
““ Like that of a lioness whom naturally crave sheep,
““ As the desire of a dolphin is in the sea,
“” A pool seizes wild oxen, as a spring does the like to hinds.


““ When I have the appetite for an ass, here, with both my hands, I eat it,
““ But, only If my sevenfold portions are served unto me,
““ Or if the cup is mixed by Nahar, the River.


““ Ba’al has invited me with my brothers, Hadad has called me with my kinsfolk,
““ But, it is to eat bread with my brothers and to drink wine with my kinsfolk.


“” Have you then forgotten, Ba’al, that I can surely transfix you?
“” I can decimate you! 


““ For all that you smote!
“” If thou goest fighting Lotan, the Evasive Serpent, if he is finished off,
“ If now thou wouldst try to destroy the wriggling, twisting, tortuous slant,
“ the serpent, The Tyrant Shalyat of Seven Heads,
“” The heavens will burn up and droop helpless,
“” For I myself will crush you in pieces!


“” Indeed, you must come down!


“” You will surely descend into the throat of El's son, Divine Mot, Death,
“” Into the watery depths, the gorge of the Beloved of El, the Youth Ghazir the Hero!"


“” One lip down to the earth, one lip to the heavens,
“” Mot stretches his tongue to the stars.
“” Ba’al must enter his maw; and must descend; like an olive-stuffed bread,
“” Like the produce of earth, the sweetling fruit of the trees.””


Ba’al was afraid. Terrified, was the Rider of Clouds:


“ Begone! 


“ Say unto Godly Mot; repeat unto El’s Beloved Ghazir, 
“ Message of Puissant Ba’al, word of the Powerful Hero:


“ Be gracious, O’ Godly Mot, thy slave I am, 
“ I am thy bondman forever!”
                        
They tarried not. Unto Godly Mot, into his city Hamriya, they came. Down to the throne that he sits on, his filthy land of inheritance, they lifted up their voice and cried:


“ Message of Puissant Son Baal, word of the Powerful Hero:


“” Be gracious, O’ Godly Mot, thy slave I am,
“” I am thy bondman forever””


The Godly Mot rejoiced:


" How shall Ba'al go on insulting me?
“ Why is Hadad the Thunderer scared out of his wits!


“ Those that fight with me are always laid low, as with a butcher's knife
“ I smite them that would smite me!"


The messengers Gapn and Ugar returned with the word of Mot. Ba’al was left trembling at the news. 


Kothar made his voice heard and addressed his words to Mighty Ba’al:


“ Thy seat is great, Mighty Ba’al, great is the seat that bears your weight,
“ It shall belong to your son.
“ Your house shall be bright, like the stars of the heavens.”


Ba’al gave answer:


“ Truly, I must call Mot, I must invite the Beloved One within the palace.
“ How can I truly put him below? 
“ Can I overturn his great strength?


“ Gather and go, Sheger! Go, Gods Ithm, gather your herd by the thousands ,
“ And seek the Divine Mot!”


They set their faces and went. The Gods of the Herd went below and sated the Godly Mot with an offering. Up, then went the Godly Mot to the mansion of Mighty Ba’al. 


He joined the feast of his kindred. 


A throne was brought and he sat. 


His eyes darted about the place. They moved to seek Mighty Ba’al. The slayer of hundreds, his eyes did seek. He lifted up his voice and cried:


“ Where then is Ba’al the Mighty? Where is Hadad the Storm?”


Ba’al arose with his seven pages, his eight serving maids. Going near the Beloved of El, he supplied him food. While the gods did eat and drink, they were supplied with a suckling. With a salted knife carved a fatling and drank flagons of wine.


From cups of gold, they drank the blood of trees; from cups of silver, the blood of vines. Flagons and goblets they poured! 


Then, up went Mot and the gods. They went up and called for new wine. 


New wine was poured.


The House of El revolted! 


They made Mot a ruler, who then sent Ba’al fleeing. He sent the god to the hills. Then, Mot bellowed:


“ Do you recount your words, the revile you spoke?”


Kothar-Hasis bade the Mighty Ba’al to abandon his home:


“ Mightiest Ba’al, take in the hand your torch, the red ochre in your right hand,
“ I shall take the life of a calf, 
“ I will put him in a hole of the earth-gods, the numinous dead. 
“ And as for you, take your clouds, your winds, your thunder-bolts, and your rains;
“ Take them with you!


“ Take with you, your seven pages, your eight noble serving maids;
“ Take with thee Padriya, daughter of Ar, with thee, Tatalliya, daughter of Rabb
“ There now, be off on thy way, unto the Mount of Kankaniya.


“ Lift the mount upon thy hands, the elevation upon thy palms.
“ Descend to the depth of the earth and you will know nothingness, like mortals,
“ For thou wilt have become as one who has died.”


Mightiest Ba’al obeyed!


Ba’al Tablet X: Ba’al and Anath


“ Is Ba’al in his house? Is God Hadd in the midst of his palace?”


The lads of Ba’al made answer:


“ Ba’al is not in his house; The God Hadd is there not, in the midst of his palace
“ His bow he has taken in his hand, his darts in his right hand.
“ He is off on his way to Shimak-Canebrake the Buffalo-Filled.”


The Maiden Anath lifted her wings.


She sped in flight to Shimak-Canebrake the Buffalo-Filled. 


Puissant Ba’al lifted up his eyes. 


As he did lift up his eyes, he beheld the Maiden Anath, fairest among Ba’al’s sisters. Before her he rose. At her feet he knelt and fell down. 


Ba’al raised up his voice and cried:


“ Hail sister! 


“ The horns of thy heifer, O’ Maiden Anath, Ba’al will anoint them in flight.
“ We’ll thrust my foes into the earth, to the ground, them that rise against thy brother!”


The Maiden Anath lifted up her eyes. She lifted up her eyes and beheld a cow. She proceeded a-walking.


She proceeded a-dancing! She danced in the pleasant spots, in the lovely places.


He seized and held her womb. She seized and held his stones. Ba’al turned to an ox. The Maiden Anath turned heifer. He kissed her there, to conceive and bear.


Calves of the cows drop. An ox for Maiden Anath. A heifer for Yahamat-Liimmim. 


Ba’al scooped his hands full, the God Hadd, his fingers full. 


He left the mouth of Maiden Anath, the mouth of his fairest sister. Ba’al went under; in the mountain. 


Dagon’s son of the sky, Ba’al, would sit upon his throne. Dagon’s son would be upon his seat.


And so she went to Arar, up to Arar and Zaphon (Arar-Zaphon). In the pleasance, the mount of possession, she cried aloud to Ba’al:


“ Receive, Ba’al, godly tidings!
“ Ye’! Receive, O’ son of Dagon:
“ A wild ox is born to Ba’al! A buffalo to the Rider of Clouds!”


Mightiest Ba’al heard. 


Puissant Ba’al rejoiced!


He made love with a heifer in the outback, a cow in a field, death’s realm. Seventy-seven, eighty-eight times he had mounted her, and she conceived and bore a boy. He was by Mightiest Ba’al, who presented him, like a gift, to his mother’s breast.


Ba’al Tablet XI: Death of Baal


Gapn and Ugar, the messengers twain, they penetrated the Mountain of El. They entered the massif, the tent of the king, Father of Years. 


Lifting their voices, they cried:


“ We two did go round to the edges of the earth, to the limits of the watery region.
“ We arrived at  the pleasant place, the desert pasture,
“ The pleasure, the land of pastures,
“ “The pleasance of Dabr-land,
“ The lovely fields of the shore of the realm of death,
“ The beauty of Shahalmamat-Field.


“ We came upon Ba’al, fallen and sunk into the earth.
“ We came upon Ba’al fallen to the ground!


“ Dead is Ba’al the Powerful!
“ Perished is the prince, Master of the Earth!"


The news reached the ears of El, Father of Shunem. Thereupon, the God of Mercy went down from the throne; he sat on the footstool, and from the footstool sat on the earth. 


He poured the ashes of grief on his head. He set the dust of wallowing on his pate. For clothing, he was covered with a doubled cloak. He roamed the mountain in mourning. 


Ye’, through the forest, he went in grief. He cut cheek and chin; then lacerated his forearms. He ploughed his chest like a garden. Like a vale, he lacerated his back.


He lifted his voice to shout:


" Ba’al is dead! Woe to the people of Dagon's son!
“ Woe to the multitudes! 
“ I shall go down unto the earth."


Anath too went. She tread every mountain to the midst of the earth, every hill in the midst of the fields. She came to the goodness of the land of Deber, the beauty of the fields of Shechelmemet. 


She came upon Ba’al prostrate on the earth. 


For clothing she was covered with a doubled cloak. The mountains in mourning, she roamed. In grief, through the forest, she cut cheek and chin, and lacerated her forearms. She ploughed her chest like a garden. Like a vale, she lacerated her back:


" Ba’al is dead! 
“ Woe to the people of Dagon's son!
“ Woe to the multitudes!
“ Let us go down into the earth."


With her, went down the Torch of the Gods, Shapash. Until she was sated with weeping, she drank tears like wine.


Aloud she cried to the Torch of the Gods, Shapash:


" Load Mighty Ba’al on to me."


Shapash hearkened. 


She lifted Ba’al on the shoulders of Anath, who raised him into the heights of Saphon. They wept for him, and buried him. 


Anath put him in the Grave of the Gods of the Earth.


She sacrificed seventy buffalo as an offering. She sacrificed seventy oxen as an offering. She sacrificed seventy head of small cattle as an offering. She sacrificed seventy deer for God Hadd. She sacrificed seventy wild goats and seventy asses as an offering for Aliyan Ba’al. 


Then, she set her face toward El, at the source of the two rivers. In the midst of the streams of the two deeps, she entered the abode. She went into the domicile of the king, Father Shunem, and at the feet of El she fell prostrate. She honoured him.


She raised up her voice:


" Let Asherah and her sons rejoice; the Goddess Elat and the band of her brood,
“ For dead is Ba’al!
“ Perished is the prince, Lord of Earth!"


Aloud cried El to Asherah of the Sea:


" Hear, O’ Lady Asherah of the Sea! 
“ The time has come to give one of thy sons, that I may make him king!"


Lady Asherah of the Sea replied:


" Let us make king, one who knows how to govern, yes. 
“ Let us make king who has knowledge and intelligence, 
“ Yadi‘-Yalhan!"




The God of Mercy declared:


" One feeble of frame will not vie with Ba’al,
“ Nor wield a spear like Dagon's Son when the time comes."


When the parley was finished, Lady Asherah of the Sea declared:


" Let us make Athtar the Awesome king!
“ Let Athtar reign!"


Thereupon Athtar the Tyrant, went into the heights of Saphon, so that he may sit on the throne. His feet did not reach the footstool, nor did his head reach its top.


Athtar the Tyrant said:


" I cannot rule in the heights of Saphon!"


Athtar the Terrible went down from the throne. He descended from the seat of Mightiest Ba’al, that he may rule over all the grand earth. 


One day passed, then two. 


Anath went, now to face Mot. With the heart of a cow toward her calf; with the heart of an ewe toward her lamb, so is the heart of Anath toward Ba’al. 


She seized Mot, ripping his garment! 


She drew close in on him, while tearing his clothes.


Her voice lifted into shouts:


" Come, Mot, yield my brother!"


And the God Mot, replied:


" What does thou ask, ‘O Virgin Anath?


Mot sneered: 


“ I was going, and roaming; every mountain to the midst of the earth,
“ Every hill to the midst of the fields.
“ My appetite gave desire for human masses
“ I hungered for souls of the multitudes of the earth.
“ I arrived at the goodness of the land of Debar,
“ The beauty of the fields of Shechelmemet.


“ I met him there, and I made him like a lamb in my mouth.
“ Like a kid in my jaws, he was crushed!"


The Torch of the Gods Shapash had her lamp-light dimmed. The heavens stopped on account of the God Mot. 


The heavens were wearied by the hand of the Beloved of ‘El, Mot.


A day passed. Then two.


From days to months.


With the heart of a cow toward her calf, with the heart of an ewe toward her lamb, so is the heart of Anath toward Ba’al. 


She seized the God Mot. 


With a sword she cleaved him!


With a pitchfork she winnowed him!


 With a fire she burned him!


In the millstones she ground him!


In the fields she planted him!


So much so, that the birds did not eat his flesh, nor did the fowl destroy any of his portion.


Yet, flesh called to flesh.


Father Shunem finally declared the God Ba’al lost:


" Perished is the prince, Lord of Earth; 
“ If Aliyan Ba’al is alive,
“ If the prince, Lord of Earth, exists, then, in a dream of the God of Mercy,
“ In a vision of the Creator of Creatures, let the heavens rain oil,
“ Let the wadies run with honey, that I may know that Aliyan Ba’al is alive,
“ That the prince, Lord of Earth, exists!"


Ba’al Tablet XI: Absence in Kurungi; Absence in Sheol


In a dream of the God of Mercy, in a vision of the Creator of Creatures, the heavens rained oil. The wadies ran with honey. 


The God of Mercy rejoiced! 


He set his feet on the footstool. He cracked a smile and laughed. He let loose a shout in relief:


" Let me sit and rest; let my soul repose in my breast.
“ For Aliyan Ba’al is alive,
“ The prince, Lord of Earth, exists."


Aloud Beneficent El loosed shout to the Virgin Anath:


" Hear, O Virgin Anath; alert the Torch of the Gods Shapash”


“ Over the furrows of the fields, O’ Shapash,
“ Over the furrows of the fields, let El set thee, for the Lord of the Ploughed Furrows,
“ Where is Aliyan Ba’al?
“ Where is the prince, Lord of Earth?”


The Virgin Anath departed, with haste.


She set face toward the Torch of the Gods, Shapash, and lifted her voice:


" The message of Bull El, thy father; the word of the God of Mercy, thy father:


“” Over the furrows of the fields, O’ Shapash,
“” Over the furrows of the fields, let El set thee, for the Lord of the Ploughed Furrows,
“” Where is Aliyan Ba’al?
“” Where is the prince, Lord of Earth?'"


The Torch of the Gods, Shapash, replied:


" I shall seek Aliyan Ba’al for you!"


The Virgin Anath answered:


" Tis not for me, O’ Shapash, tis not I, but Bull El who summons thee.
“ May the gods guard thee in Sheol!"


Ba’al Tablet XII: Children of Asherah 


Kindly ‘El the Benign spoke:


" Parched are the furrows of the fields, O’ Shapash!
“ Dried are the furrows of El's fields!
“ Ba’al has neglected the furrows of his tillage.


“ Where is Mighty Ba’al?
“ Where is the prince, the Master of the Earth?"


The Virgin Anat, again, departed. Indeed she set her face toward the Luminary of the Gods', the torch, Lady Shapash, the sun. She lifted up her voice and cried:


" Message of the Bull, El your father:


“" Parched are the furrows of the fields, O’ Shapash!
“” Dried are the furrows of El's fields!
“” Ba'al has neglected the furrows of his tillage.


“” Where is Mighty Ba'al?
“” Where is the Prince, the Master of the Earth?””


The Luminary of the Gods' replied. Lady Shapash, the sun, then said:


" Pour sparkling wine from its container; bring a garland for your sibling.
“ Let the children of your family wear wreaths, I will seek Mighty Ba’al."


The Virgin Anat answered:


" Hence and thither you go, O’ Shapash,
“ Wherever and whither, may El protect you!"


Together, Anat, Baalat Shamim Rammim, and Lady Shapash, went searching for Baal, to return him to his palace. Below he was found, in Mot’s domain, the Mire. 


Mighty Ba’al was seized in sleeping. 


Ye’, now Ba’al would make fertile with his rain!


With water, he indeed made the harrowed land fertile. He put his voice in the clouds. His flashing lightning went to the earth. 


Ba’al seized the sons of Athirat. Rabbim, the Great Ones, he smote with the broad-sword. He smote them in the back, and on the shoulder.


Dokyamim, the Pounders of the Sea, the Makers of Waves, he smote with the mace. Tsorim, the Small Ones, he dragged to the ground of the earth. 


Ba’al sat upon the throne of his kingship. He sat on the cushion on the seat of his dominion.


A day, then, days passed. From days to months. From months to years. 


In the seventh year, the Godly Mot spoke. He lifted up his voice and cried:


“ Because of thee, Ba’al, I have experienced abasement!
“ Because of thee, I have experienced splitting with the sword!
“ Because of thee, I have experienced burning with fire!
“ Because of thee, I have experienced grinding with mill-stones!
“ I have experienced winnowing, sifting with the basket, the sieve!
“ I have experienced sowing in the fields!
“ And, I have experienced scattering in the sea!


“ Give one of your brothers so that I may be sat down 
“ And that I may eat. The anger that I feel will turn back.


“ If not one of your brothers, behold I shall annihilate the earth
“ I shall consume humankind; and I shall make an end to the multitudes of earth!”


Ba’al stood, defiantly, raised his voice and gave shout:


“ I will drive him forth
“ I will banish him, Divine Mot, son of El,
“ Death! Devourer of Peoples!
“ Let him eat his seven pages!”


So it was ordered.


Answered Divine Mot:


" But look! 


“ Ba’al has given me my own brothers to eat!
“ The sons of my mother, to consume!"


Mot returned to Ba’al in the heights of Zaphon. 


He lifted up his voice and he cried:


" My own brothers you have given me, Ba’al!
“ Woe, to eat! 
“ The sons of my mother to consume!"


They butted each other like antelopes! 


Mot was firm. Ba’al was firm. 


They gored, like wild oxen! 


Mot was strong. Ba’al was strong. 


They bit like serpents! 


Mot was firm. Ba’al was firm. 


They kicked like chargers! 


Mot fell down. Ba’al fell down over him.


Lady Shapash, the sun, cried out from above to Mot:


" Hear! 
“ I beseech thee, O’ Divine Mot, El’s son;
“ How can you battle with Mighty Ba’al?


“  Beware lest thy father Bull El, hear thee; 
“ Surely he will undermine thee, and pull up the foundations of your seat.
“ Indeed, he will overturn the throne of your kingship,
“ Smash the sceptre of your dominion.’


Divine Mot was afraid. The Beloved of El, the Youth Ghazir the Hero, was terrified. Mot roused himself at her call. He arose from his prostration, lifted up his voice and cried:


“ Let them seat Ba’al on the throne of his kingdom.
“ Let him sit on the cushion, on the seat of his dominion.


“ Do you indeed betake yourself to the fresh meat?
“ Yes, do you indeed eat the bread of the sacrificial meal?
“ Yes, do you indeed drink the wine of oblation?


“ Lady Shapash, the shades are under you, 
“ Your judges shall govern the gathered ones, the Rapha'im;
” Lady Shapash, the ghosts are under you   
“ The gods, your judges, shall hold sway over the upper gods, Ilnym;
“ The gods shall come to you!


“ Behold! 


“ The dead-gods come to you!
“ Death is yours!
“ Kothar is your friend and your intimate companion, Khasis,


“ In the sea are Arsh, Desire, and Tannin the Dragon;
“ Let Kothar wa-Khasis banish them 
“ Let Kothar wa-Khasis drive them away!”


On the sea of monsters and dragons, proceeded Kothar wa-Khasis. 


Kothar wa-Khasis journeyed.


Elkunirsa and Ashertu:


Ba’al came to the house of Elkunirsa. Ba’al went through, and a messenger greeted him there, at the gate: 


“" Stay behind me, and I will stay behind you. 
““ Else, I will press you down with my word and stab you with my spindle.
““ I will move you to the netherworld.”" 


Ba’al heard, stood up, and went to the headwaters of the Euphrates River. He went to Elkunirsa, the husband of Ashertu, and entered into his tent. 


Elkunirsa looked at Ba’al and asked him: 


" Why have you come?" 


Ba’al answered: 


" When I came into your house, 
“ Ashertu sent young women to me, saying: 


“” Come sleep with me.”” 


“ I refused. 


“ Then, she threatened me and spoke thus: 


“” Stay behind me, and I will stay behind you.
“” Else, I will press you down with my word and stab you with my spindle.'” 


“ That is why I have come, my father. 
“ I did not come to you in the person of a messenger; I myself have come to you. 


“ Ashertu is rejecting you, her own husband. 
“ Although she is your wife, yet she keeps sending to me:


“” Sleep with me.'" 


“Do you not notice? She is impugning your virility.”


Elkunirsa replied to Ba’al: 


" Go,
“ Do what you will to her, Ashertu, my wife,
“ Go and humble her." 


Ba’al heard the words of Elkunirsa and went to Ashertu. 


He said to Ashertu: 


" I have killed your seventy-seven children. 
“ Your eighty-eight I have killed." 


When Ashertu heard the humiliating report, her mind within her became sad. She appointed mourning women, and she lamented for seven years. Then, Elkunirsa and Ashertu ate and drank for them. 


When the gods were sated, and had drunk their fill, Ashertu rose. She stood up and said to Elkunirsa: 


" Now, I will press Ba’al down with my words. 
“ With my spindle I will stab him and I will sleep with you." 


Elkunirsa heard and said to his wife:


" Come take Ba’al as your prisoner, and do to him as you wish." 


Anat-Astarte, too, heard those words. 


She was a cup in Elkunirsa's hand. He became drunk, and she became an owl and perched on his wall. 


Anat-Astarte heard the words which husband and wife spoke to one another. None could notice her.


Elkunirsa and his wife went to her bed and slept together. Anat-Astarte flew off like a bird across the desert. 


There, in the desert she found Ba’al and said to him: 


" O’ Ba’al, do not eat the bread of Ashertu,
“ Do not drink wine together,
“ Do not go toward her house,
“ She is seeking to capture you." 


Ba’al set his face towards his own house. To Mount Saphon, he went to warn his wife and children. He entered the house, but was greeted by silence. 


Then, she pressed down on him with her words; she stabbed him with her spindle. Ba’al was shaken. His frame bent and to his knees, he fell.


Anat-Astarte followed close behind. For fear she moved to Ba’al’s house, with the wind upon her feet as shoes. There, she found him, his body lay at the gate.


From the dark earth, the gods heard the words and gave heed. They held him by his members, and to him, the Dark Gods, the Netherworld Deities, dredged up sleep. The Goddess Anat-Astarte said to the Netherworld Deities: 


" When his penis, tendons, muscles, and members become stiff; 
“ When he is filthy with excrement, I will proceed to give him to you.


“ Release him or my fury will become worse!
“ Why has he sent a living persons to the Dark Earth?" 


They seized Ba’al's members, they came upon his calves like a snake. They dragged them down to the dark earth, and thus said: 


" Lord Ba’al, his wife, and children went down into the Dark Earth.
“ They set up dwelling there"


Maiden Anat-Astarte began to say: 


" Do not take him! 
“ I will conduct the rites and the Gods of the Dark Earth, they shall come and see!”


From the Dark Earth, up they went to Ba’al. Anat-Astarte called the Mother Goddesses to him. 


There, they re-created Ba’al; like a torch they made him radiant.


The exorcists went to Ba’al. A man from Amurru, and a man from Anaharat, and the chief of the Shunem men. They exorcised him and purified him from oath, offense, sin, evil word and anger. 


Ba’al's body became pure radiant. 


Anat-Astarte said to the Gods of the Dark Earth:


" The gods have recreated Ba’al 
“ They have brought him back up from the dark earth


“ If he asks when you go to him, tell him of the choice I made”


Then, the Netherworld Deities went to Elkunirsa; and word, they brought. 


Anat-Astarte went and conducted her steps, appointed her vengeance. 


Elkunirsa, his one child, Ashertu, her one child, the last remaining child, she made sleepy. 


Then, she came upon Elkunirsa.


She took the parts from his internals, those too from his head, and a hair. She took sight from his eyes. She took hearing from his ears. She took touch from his hands . She took the waters from his body.


The life from his body, she took. 
The Contendings of Horus and Seth




Then came to pass, the adjudging of Horus and Seth. Those twain, mysterious of form, great, mightiest princes that ever came into being. 


A certain little child sat before the Master of the Universe claiming the kingly office of his father, Osiris, of him beautiful of appearings, the son of Ptah, who enlighteneth the netherworld with his comeliness. Thoth was presenting the eye to the mighty prince who was in Heliopolis.


Then spake Shu, the son of Re, before Atum, the mighty prince, who is in Heliopolis:


“ All justice is powerful! 
“ Do it, saying:


“ Give the kingly office unto Horus.”  


Then spake Thoth unto the Divine Ennead:


“ Right, 
“ A million times, 
“ Right!” 


Isis uttered a great cry, and rejoiced exceedingly. She came and stood before the Master of the Universe, and she said: 


“ North wind, hie to the west, and bear the good tidings to Onnophris.”  


Then spake  Shu, the son of Re : 


 “ The presenting of the eye is the justice of the Ennead!” 


Then said the Master of the Universe:


“ What signifieth your taking action yourselves, alone?”


The Ennead said:


“ He hath taken the royal name-ring of Horus,
“ They have set the white crown upon his head.” 


Then, was the Master of the Universe silent, being angry with the Ennead. 


Thereupon, Seth, the son of Nut, said: 


“ Let him be cast forth  with me, that I may cause thee to see,
“ My hand prevailing over his hand, in presence of the Ennead,
“ For none knoweth any means of dispossessing him.”


But Thoth spoke unto him:


“ We shall not be able to discern the guilty one.
“ Shall the office of Osiris be given to Seth, while his son Horus is alive?” 


And Pre-Harakhti was exceedingly angry. For it was the wish of Pre to give the kingly office unto Seth, great of strength, the son of Nut. 


Onuris uttered a great cry before the face of the Ennead, saying:


“ What are we to do?” 


Thereupon, Atum, the mighty prince who is in Heliopolis, said: 


“ Let Banebdjedet the Great God, the living, be summoned,
“ In order that he may pronounce judgement upon the two striplings.”


So they brought Banebdedet the Great God, who dwelleth in Setit, before Atum, together with Ptah-tanen. And he said unto them:


“ Pronounce judgement upon the two striplings,
“ And stop them from standing thus and wrangling every day.”


Banebdedet the Great God, the living, made answer to that which was said: 


“ Let not us take action in our ignorance,
“ But cause to be sent a letter unto Neith the Mighty, the god’s mother.
“ What she shall say, that will we do.”


 Thereupon, the Ennead spake unto Banebdedet the Great God, the living:


“ Judgement was made between them in the primeval time, in the hall Alone-of-Truths.”


The Ennead spake unto Thoth in the presence of the Master of the Universe:


“ Make thou a letter unto Neith the Mighty, the god’s mother, 
“ In the name of the Master of the Universe,
“ The bull which dwelleth in Heliopolis.” 


Thoth said: 


“I will do so, verily I will do so!”


 Thereupon, he sat down to make the letter, and he said:


“ The king of upper and lower Egypt, Re-Atum, the beloved of Thoth,
“ The lord of the two lands in Heliopolis, 
“ The sun which enlighteneth the two lands with his comeliness,
“ The Nile mighty in taking possession, 
“ Re-Harakhti!
“ Whilst Neith the Mighty, the God’s Mother,
“ Who enlightened the first face, still liveth, is in health, and flourisheth,
“ The living manifestation of  the Master of the Universe, 
“ The bull in Heliopolis, as the good king of To-meri,
“ To this effect:


“ I, thy servant spend the night in thought for Osiris,
“ Taking counsel with the two lands every day, while Sobk endureth forever. 


“ What are we to do unto the two men,
“ Who these eighty years past have been before the tribunal, 
“ None knoweth how to pronounce judgement upon them twain.
“ Mayst thou write unto us what we shall do.”


Thereupon Neith the Mighty, the God’s Mother, sent a letter to the Ennead, saying:


“ Give the office of Osiris to his son Horus, 
“ And do not do those great acts of wickedness which are not in their place.
“ Else, I shall be angry, and the heaven shall crash to the ground.
“ And let it be spoken unto the Master of the Universe,
“ The bull which dwelleth in Heliopolis,
“ Double thou, Seth, in his possessions.
“ Give unto him Anat and Astarte thy two daughters,
“ And set thou Horus in the place of his father Osiris.” 


The letter of Neith the Mighty, the God’s Mother, reached the Ennead as they sat in the hall, Horus-Prominent-of-Horns. The letter was put into the hand of Thoth. He read it out before the Master of the Universe and before the entire Ennead. 


Then, they spake with one mouth: 


“ This goddess is in the right.” 


Thereupon the Master of the Universe was angry with Horus, and he said unto him:


“ Thou art feeble in thy limbs, and this kingly office is too great for thee,
“ Thou stripling, the taste of whose mouth is bad!” 


Onuris was angry a million times, and so was the entire Ennead, even the Thirty. The god, Babai, rose up and he spake unto Pre-Harakhti: 


“ Thy shrine is empty!”


Pre-Harakhti was aggrieved at this taunt which had been spoken to him. He laid himself down upon his back, and his heart was very sore. 


The Ennead went forth. Then, they cried aloud before the face of the God Babai. And they said unto him:


“ Get thee forth, this crime that thou hast done is exceedingly great!”


And they went to their tents. 


The great god passed a day lying upon his back in his arbour. His heart was very sore, and he was alone. 


After a long time, Hathor, the Lady of the Southern Sycamore, came and stood before her father, the Master of the Universe. She uncovered her nakedness before his face, and the great god laughed at her. 


He rose up, and he sat down with the great Ennead. He said unto Horus and Seth:


“ Speak concerning yourselves.” 


Thereupon, Seth, great of strength, the son of Nut, said: 


“ As for me, I am Seth, the greatest of strength among the Ennead, 
“ I slay the enemy of Pre daily, being in front of the Bark of Millions,
“ And none other god is able to do it.
“ I am entitled to the office of Osiris.” 


In reply, they said:


“ Seth, the son of Nut, is in the right.” 


Onuris and Thoth cried aloud, saying:


“ Shall the office be given unto a brother on the side of the mother,
“ While a son of the body is yet alive?“


Then spoke Banebdedet the Great God, the living, and said: 


“ Shall the office be given unto this stripling,
“ While Seth, his elder brother, is yet alive?”


The Ennead cried aloud before the face of the Master of the Universe, and they said unto him:


“ What are these words that thou hast spoken,
“ Which are not worthy, that they should be heard?”


 Horus, the son of Isis, spoke:


“ Not good is this, in sooth, that I should be cheated in presence of the Ennead,
“ And that the office of my father, Osiris, should be taken away from me.” 


Isis was angry with the Ennead, and she made an oath to God in presence of the Ennead, saying: 


“ As my mother, the Goddess Neith, liveth, 
“ And as Ptah-tanen, high of plumes, curber of the horns of the gods, liveth,
“ These words shall be placed before Atum,
“ The mighty prince who is in Heliopolis,
“ And likewise before Khopri who dwelleth in his bark.” 


The Ennead replied to her: 


“ Be not vexed, his rights shall be given unto him who is in the right,
“ And all that which thou hast said shall be done.”


Seth, the son of Nut, was angry with the Ennead. When they said these words to Isis the Mighty, the god’s mother. Seth spoke unto them: 


“ I will take my sceptre of four thousand and five hundred pounds,
“ And I will kill one of you each day.”


Seth made an oath to the Master of the Universe, saying: 


“ I will not contend in the tribunal whilst Isis is in it.”


And Pre-Harakhti spoke unto them:


“ Cross ye over to the Island-in-the-Midst,
“ And judge ye between them,


“ Say unto Anti the Ferryman.
“ Do not ferry across any woman in the semblance of Isis.”


Then, the Ennead crossed over to the Island-in-the-Midst. 


They sat down and ate bread.


Isis went, and she drew nigh unto Anti, the ferryman, as he sat anigh his boat. She had changed herself into an aged woman, and she went along and bowed down. A little ring of gold was on her finger when she spoke unto him:


“ I have come unto thee that thou mayst ferry me across to the Island-in-the-Midst,
“ For I have come with this jar of flour for the little lad. 
“ He has been looking after some cattle in the Island-in-the-midst 
“ For five days unto today.
“ He is hungry.” 


And Anti spake unto her:


“ It was said unto me. Do not ferry across any woman!”


In reply, she spoke unto him:


“ Was it said to thee on account of Isis, this that thou hast said?” 


He did not answer, but spoke thus: 


“ What wilt thou give me, that I may ferry thee across to the Island-in-the-Midst?”


Isis replied to him :


“ I will give thee this loaf.”


Thereupon, he spoke unto her, angrily: 


“ What is it to me, thy loaf?
“ Shall I ferry thee across to the Island-in-the-Midst,
“ When it has been said to me:


“” Ferry no woman across”” 


“ For the sake of thy loaf?“ 


She spoke again:


“ I will give thee this ring of gold which is in my hand.” 


And he answered: 


“ Let me have the ring of gold!” 


And she gave it to him. 


Thereupon, he ferried her across to the Island-in-the-Midst. 


While she was going beneath the trees, she looked and saw the Ennead. They sat and ate bread, in the presence of the Master of the Universe, in his arbour.


Seth looked and saw her. She was coming there from afar off. She uttered an incantation with her magic, and changed herself into a maiden fair of her limbs. 


There was not the like of her in the entire land. 


Seth loved her right sore. 


Thereupon, he rose up, sat down, and ate bread with the great Ennead. Then, he went to overtake her. No one had seen her except him. He stood behind a tree, and he cried unto her, as he said: 


“ I am here with thee, fair maiden.” 


And she spoke unto him:


“ Nay, my great lord!


“ As for me, I was the wife of a herdsman of cattle, and I bore unto him a male child. 
“ My husband died, and the stripling came to be after the cattle of his father.
“ Then a foreigner came.
“ He sat down in my byre, and thus spoke he unto my son: 


“” I will beat thee, and I will take away the cattle of thy father, 
“” I will cast thee forth.”” 


“ Thus spoke unto him.
“ But, my wish is to make thee act for him, as champion.” 


And Seth spoke unto her: 


“ Shall the cattle be given to the foreigner, while the son of the goodman is alive?”


Isis changed herself into a kite, and she flew. She perched on the top of an acacia and called unto Seth, saying to him:


“ Weep for thyself! 
“ It is thine own mouth that hath said it,
“ It is thine own cleverness which hath adjudged thee.


“ What aileth thee now?” 


And he stood a-weeping. 


He went to the place where Pre-Harakhti  was, and he wept. 


Thereupon, Pre-Harakhti spoke unto him:


“ What aileth thee now?”


Seth spoke unto him: 


“ That evil woman came against me again,
“ That she might beguile me once again,


“ Having changed herself into a fair maiden before my face.
“ She spoke unto me: 


“” As for me, I was the wife of a herdsman of cattle,
“” He died, and I did bare to him a male child.
“” He is after some cattle belonging to his father.


“” A foreigner visited my byre and that of my son, and I gave him bread.
“” After many days following upon these things the stranger I spake unto my son: 


“”” I will beat thee, and I will take away the cattle of thy father,
“”” And they shall become mine.””” 


“” Thus he spake unto my son””


“ So she said to me.”


Thereupon Pre-Harakhti spake unto him: 


“ And what saidst thou to her?” 


Seth spoke:
 
“ I said to her: 


“ Shall the cattle be given to the foreigner,
“ While the son of the goodman is yet alive?


“ So I said to her.


“ One shall beat the face of the stranger with a stick, 
“ And he shall be cast forth, and thy son shall be set in the place of his father


“ So I said to her.”


Pre-Harakhti then spoke unto him:


“ But now look thee, thou hast adjudged thine own self! 


“ What aileth thee now?” 


And Seth spoke: 


“ Let Anti, the ferryman, be brought, 
“ And let a great punishment be inflicted upon him, saying.
“ Why didst thou let her cross?
“ So shall it be said to him.” 


Thereupon, Anti the Ferryman was brought before the Ennead. They removed the soles of his feet and Anti forswore gold unto this day. In presence of the Great Ennead, he spake, saying: 


“ Gold hath been made unto me into an abomination for my city!” 


The Ennead crossed over to the western tract, and sat down upon the mountain. When it was eventide, Pre-Harakhti and Atum, the Lord of the Two Lands in Heliopolis, sent unto the Ennead, saying:


“ What are ye doing still sitting here? 


“ As for these two striplings, ye will cause them to end their lives in the tribunal!
“ When my letter reacheth you,
“ Ye shall set the white crown upon the head of Horus, the son of Isis,
“ Ye shall promote him to the place of his father, Osiris.” 


Seth was angry, and right sore. 


And the Ennead spoke unto Seth:


“ Why art thou angry? 
“ Shall one not do as Atum, the Lord of the Two Lands in Heliopolis,
“ And Pre-Harakhti have said?” 


They established the white crown upon the head of Horus, the son of Isis. Seth cried aloud before the face of the Ennead, and he was vexed. 


He said thus:


“ Shall the office be given to my little brother,
“ While I, his elder brother, am yet alive?”


He made an oath, saying:


“ They shall remove the white crown from the head of Horus, the son of Isis,
“ They shall cast him into the water,
“ That I may contend with him regarding the office of ruler.” 


Pre-Harakhti did accordingly. 


Seth spoke unto Horus:


“ Come, let us change ourselves into two hippopotamuses,
“ Let us plunge into the waters which are in the great-green.
“ And whoso shall emerge within the period of three months,
“ To him shall this office not be given.” 


Thereupon, those twain plunged in. Isis sat a-weeping, and saying: 


“ Seth hath killed Horus, my son.” 


She took a quantity of yarn and made a cord. She took a pound of copper, and melted it down into weapons of the water. She tied the rope to it, and threw it into the water, in the place of the plunging in which Horus and Seth had made. 


The barb bit into the majesty of her son, Horus. 


He cried aloud, saying: 


“ Come unto me, my mother Isis!
“ My mother! 
“ Call unto thy barb, that it loose from me, for I am Horus, the son of Isis.” 


Isis cried aloud, and she said unto the barb:


“ Loose thou from him; behold, he is my son Horus, my child.” 


Her barb loosed from him, and she threw it again, into the water. It bit into the Majesty of Seth. 


He cried aloud, saying:


“ What have I done against thee, my sister Isis?
“ Call unto thy barb that it loose from me,
“ For I am thy brother on the side of the mother, O’ Isis.” 


Then she had compassion upon him exceedingly. 


Seth called unto her, saying:


“ Didst thou love the foreigner?
“ More than thou dost thy brother on the side of the mother?
“ Even Seth?”  


So, Isis called unto her barb, saying:


“ Loose from him; behold,
“ It is the brother of Isis on the side of the mother into whom thou hast bitten.” 


Thereupon, the barb loosed from him.


Horus, the son of Isis, was angry with his mother. He went forth, and his face was savage like a panther of Upper Egypt. His chopper of sixteen pounds was in his hand. 


Horus removed the head of his mother Isis, and put it in his bosom. Then, he ascended into the mountain. 


Thereupon, Isis changed herself into a statue of flint which had no head.


Pre-Harakhti spoke unto Thoth: 


“ What is this female that has come, and she hath no head?” 


Thoth answered unto Pref-Harakhti:


“ O’ my good lord, this is Isis the Mighty, the God’s Mother,
“ Horus, her son, hath removed her head!” 


Pre-Harakhti cried out aloud, and said unto the Ennead: 


“ Let us go and inflict a great punishment upon him.”


The Ennead ascended into the mountains in order to search for Horus, the son of Isis. 


Horus, was lying under a shenusha-tree in the oasis country. Seth found him, and took hold on him. He threw him on his back upon the mountain, and removed his two eyes from their places. He buried them upon the mountain in order to illumine the earth. 


The two balls of his two eyes became two bulbs, and they grew into lotuses. 


Thereupon, Seth returned, and spoke unto Pre-Harakhti, falsely:


“ I did not find Horus!”


Although he had found him! 


Hathor, the Lady of the Southern Sycamore, went, and she found Horus. He lay a-weeping in the desert. 
She took hold of a gazelle, and milked it. 


She then spoke unto Horus:


“ Open thine eye, that I may put this milk therein.” 


He opened his eyes, and she put the milk therein. She put it in the right eye, and in the left; then, she spoke unto him:


“ Open thine eye” 


He opened his eyes, and she saw him. She found him restored. 


Thereupon, she went to speak unto Pre-Harakhti:


“ I found Horus! 
“ Seth had deprived him of his eyes,
“ So I raised him up again, and behold, he has come.”


The Ennead said:


“ Let Horus and Seth be summoned, 
“ In order that judgement may be made between them.”


They were brought before the Ennead. 


Then, spoke the Master of the Universe before the great Ennead, unto Horus and Seth:


“ Go ye, and let what I have said unto you be listened to;


“ Eat ye, and drink ye, 
“ Let us be at peace, and cease ye from wrangling thus every day.” 


Seth spoke unto Horus: 


“ Come, let us pass a happy day in my house.” 


Horus said to him: 


“ I will do so, 
“ Verily, I will do so!” 


When it was eventide, the bed was spread for them. They twain lay down, and in the night. Seth caused his member to become stiff, and made it go between the loins of Horus. 


Horus put his two hands between his loins, and caught the seed of Seth. He then went to speak unto his mother Isis:


“ Come unto me, O’ Isis, my  mother!
“ Come and see this, which Seth hath done to me!” 


He opened his hand, and caused her to see the seed of Seth. She cried out aloud, and seized her knife. 


She cut off his hand, and cast it into the water. 


She drew out for him a hand of like worth. 


Thereupon, she took a dab of sweet ointment, and put it upon the member of Horus. She caused it to become stiff, put it into a pot, and he made his seed run down into it. The next morning, Isis went with the seed of Horus to the garden of Seth.


And she spoke unto the gardener of Seth:


“ What herb is it that Seth doth eat here with thee?"


And the gardener spoke unto her: 


“ He doth not eat any herb here with me except lettuces.”


So, Isis put the seed of Horus upon them. 


Seth came after his fashion of every day, and he ate the lettuces which he regularly used to eat. And he arose pregnant with the seed of Horus. Then, Seth went to speak unto Horus: 


“ Come, let us go, in order that I may contend with thee in the tribunal.” 


Horus spoke unto him: 


“ I will do so, 
“ Verily I will do so!” 


They twain went to the tribunal, and stood before the great Ennead. 


It was said unto them: 


“ Speak concerning yourselves.” 


Thereupon, Seth said: 


“ Let there be given unto me the office of ruler, 
“ For as to Horus, this same that standeth here,
“ I have performed doughty deeds of war against him.” 


The Ennead cried out aloud, and they belched and spat before the face of Horus. Yet,  Horus laughed at them. 


Horus made an oath to the gods, saying:


“ False is all that which Seth hath said.
“ Let the seed of Seth be summoned, that we may see whence it will answer.
“ And let mine own be summoned, that we may see whence it will answer.” 


Thereupon, Thoth, the Lord of Divine Words, the Scribe of Truth of the Ennead, placed his hand upon the arm of Horus, and he said: 


“ Come forth, thou seed of Seth!” 


 It answered to him from the water in the fen. 


Thoth placed his hand upon the arm of Seth, and said: 


“ Come forth, thou seed of Horus!” 


It spoke unto him: 


“ Where shall I come forth” 


Thoth spoke unto it: 


“ Come forth from his ear!” 


Again, it spoke unto him : 


“ Shall I come forth from his ear?
“ I, who am a divine effluence?” 


Thereupon, Thoth answered unto it:


“ Come forth from his forehead!” 


And it came forth as a sun of gold upon the head of Seth.


Seth was exceedingly angry, and stretched forth his hand to lay hold on the sun of gold. Thoth took it away from him, and put it as an ornament upon his head. 


Thereupon, the Ennead said: 


“ Horus is in the right, and Seth is in the wrong.” 


Seth was exceedingly angry, and he cried aloud when they said: 


“ Horus is in the right, and Seth is in the wrong.” 


Seth made a great oath to the gods, saying:


“ They shall not give the office unto him, until he hath been cast forth with me.
“ And we will fashion for ourselves some ships of stone, and we will sail around, 
“ We twain. 
“ And whoso shall prevail over his fellow, to him shall they give the office of ruler.”  


Horus fashioned for himself a ship of cedar, and he plastered it with gypsum, and cast it upon the water at eventide.


 No man who was in the entire land had seen it. 


Seth saw the ship of Horus, and thought it was stone. He went unto the mountain, and cut off a mountain peak. He fashioned for himself a ship of stone, of one hundred and thirty and eight cubits. Then, they went down into their ships, in presence of the Ennead. 


The ship of Seth sank in the water. So, Seth changed himself into a hippopotamus, and he caused  the ship of Horus to founder. 


Horus took his barb, and he threw it at the Majesty of Seth. 


The Ennead spoke unto him:


“ Throw it not at him.” 


Thereupon, he took the weapons of the water, and placed them in his ship. He fared down to Sais to speak unto Neith the Mighty, the God’s Mother: 


“ Let judgement be pronounced upon me and Seth,
“ For as much as these eighty years we have been in the tribunal,
“ None knoweth how to pronounce judgement upon us.
“ Yet hath he not been declared in the right against me,


“ But for a thousand times before this I have been in the right against him every day.
“ Nor yet, doth he regard aught that the Ennead have said.


“ I contended with him in the hall, Way-of-Truths 
“ And I was declared in the right against him.


“ I contended with him in the hall, Horus-Prominent-of-Horns 
“ And I was declared in the right against him. 


“ I contended with him in the hall, Field-of-Reeds
“ And I was declared in the right against him.


“ I contended with him in the hall, the-Field-Pool 
“ And I was declared in the right against him.” 


The Ennead spoke unto Shu, the son of Re: 


“ Right in all that he hath said is Horus, the son of Isis.” 


Speech was made by Thoth unto the Master of the Universe:


“ Cause a letter to be sent unto Osiris,
“ That he may pronounce judgement upon the two striplings.” 


Then spoke Shu, the son of Re: 


“ Right, a million times right is that which Thoth hath spoken unto the Ennead.” 


Thereupon, the Master of the Universe spoke unto Thoth:


“ Sit down and make a letter to Osiris, that we may hear, that which he shall say.” 


Then, Thoth sat down to complete a letter unto Osiris, saying: 


“ The Bull, lion that hunteth for himself.
“ The Two-Goddesses, protecting the gods curbing the two lands.
“ Horus of Gold, inventor of men in the primeval time;
“ King of Upper and Lower Egypt.
“ Bull, which dwelleth in Heliopolis, son of Ptah, fertile of territory, 
“ Who arose as father of his Ennead!
“ He eateth of gold and all manner of rare gems. 


“ Mayst thou write unto us, that which we shall do unto Horus and Seth,
“ In order that we may not take action in our ignorance.” 


After many days, upon following these things, the letter reached the king, the son of Re, Great-of-Abundance, Lord-of-Plenty. 


He cried aloud when the letter was read out before him. 


He answered it very quickly. 


It was sent unto the place where the Master of the Universe was together with the Ennead, saying:


“ Wherefore shall my son Horus be defrauded.
“ Seeing that it is I who make you strong,
“ And it is I who made the barley and the spelt to nourish the gods
“ And even so the living creatures after the gods,
“ And no god nor any goddess found himself able to do it.” 


The letter of Osiris came unto the place where Pre-Harakhti was. He sat with the Ennead at the bright moment in Xois. It was then read out in presence of him and the Ennead. And Pre-Harakhti said:


“ Answer thou for me this letter very quickly unto Osiris,
“ Speak unto him in respect of this letter: 


” Suppose thou hadst never come into existence,
” Suppose thou hadst never been born, the barley and the spelt would still exist.”


The letter of the Master of the Universe reached Osiris, and it was read out before him. Thereupo,n he sent unto Pre-Harakhti again, saying:


“ Exceedingly good is all that thou hast done,
“ Thou inventor of the Ennead in very truth,
“ Whilst justice hath been suffered to sink within the netherworld.


“ But look, thou, at the matter thyself also!


“ As for this land in which I am, it is full of savage-faced messengers,
“ They fear not any god nor any goddess.
“ I will cause them to go forth
“ And they shall fetch the heart of whosoever doeth evil deeds,
“ They shall be here with me.


“ Moreover, what signifieth it, that I be resting here in the west,
“ Whilst ye are without, all of you!
“ Who is there among them stronger than I?


“ But, behold, they have invented falsehood in very truth.
“ Is it not so that when Ptah the Great, south of his wall, 
“ The Lord of Onkh-Towi made the sky,
“ Did he not speak unto the stars which are in it: 


“ Ye shall go to rest in the West every night in the place where King Osiris is,
“ After, the gods, nobles and plebeians shall go to rest,
“ Also in the place where thou art.
“ So said he not, unto me?” 


After many days, upon following these things, the letter of Osiris reached the place, where the Master of the Universe was together with the Ennead. 


Thereupon, Thoth received the letter, and read it out before Pre-Harakhti and the Ennead. 


They said:


“ Right in all that he hath said is 
“ He the Great-of-Abundance, Lord-of-Plenty”


Seth said: 


“ Let us be taken unto the Island-in-the-Midst
“ In order that I may contend with him.” 


And, he went unto the Island-in-the-Midst. 


Horus was declared in the right over him. 


Atum, the lord of the two lands in Heliopolis, sent unto Isis, saying:


 “ Bring thou Seth, 
“ He, being made fast with bonds.” 


Isis brought Seth, fast with bonds, as a prisoner. 


Atum spoke unto him:


“ Wherefore hast thou not allowed judgement to be pronounced upon you,
“ But hast taken away for thyself the office of Horus.”


Thereupon, Seth spoke unto him:


“ Not so, my good lord! 
“ Let Horus, the son of Isis, be summoned,
“ And let be given unto him the office of his father, Osiris.” 


They brought Horus, the son of Isis, and set the white crown upon his head. He was set in the place of his father Osiris, and they spoke unto him: 


“ Thou art the good king of To-Meri, 
“ Thou art the good lord of every land for ever and ever.”


Isis cried aloud unto her son Horus, saying:


“ Thou art the good king, 
“ My heart is in joy that thou enlightenest the earth with thy comeliness.” 


Thereupon, Ptah the Great, south of his wall, the Lord of Onkh-Towi, said:


“ What is that which shall be done unto Seth, for now behold,
“ Horus hath been set in the place of his father Osiris.”


Pre-Harakhti said:


“ Let Seth, the son of Nut, be given unto me,
“ That he may dwell with me and be as my son, 
“ He shall thunder in the sky, and men shall fear him.” 


They went to speak unto Pre-Harakhti:


“ Horus, the son of Isis, is arisen as ruler.” 


Pre was in joy exceedingly, and he spoke unto the Ennead:


“ Jubilate ye! To the ground before Horus, the son of Isis!” 


Thereupon, Isis said:


“ Horus is arisen as ruler, the Ennead is in holiday, heaven is in joy!”


Then, they took wreaths. When they saw Horus, the son of Isis, arisen as great ruler of Egypt, the Ennead and their hearts were content. The entire earth was in rejoicing when they saw Horus, the son of Isis, the office of his father Osiris, the Lord of Dedu, having been allotted unto him. 


It has come to a happy ending in Thebes, the place of truth.


Chapter 16 (Epilogue)- Åndehsyn: 
The Avestas


The Soul of Creation


The Soul of the Creation complained: 


“ For whose benefit did you fashion me? 
“ Who shaped me? 


“ Fury rages against me; violence and cruelty, maltreatment and roughness oppress me;
“ I have no herdsman, except you.
“ Therefore, it is you I beg to procure me good pasture.”


Then the Shaper of the Bovine asked Asha:


“ What was thy idea about a judge for the bovine?" 


“ Did you make an energetic herdsman along with the pasture,
“ When you made the bovine creation?" 
“ On whom have you decided as her lord?
“ Who may repel the fury of the attack by the Drujists?" 


Mazdah who was with Asha, answered to the Shaper of the Creation:


“ For the bovine, do I not know of a helper, who would not be liable to do harm? 


“ Those savages yonder do not comprehend how just men would treat their dependents.
“ But if there is no human helper, 
“ To whomsoever of living beings I come as help,
“ He shall be the strongest of living beings.”


Asha interrupted:


“ Mazdah is the being most retentive of the plans,
“ Which have been performed by Daevas and men in the past; 
“ And, also of the plans which shall be performed in the future. 


“ As to the present, it is he, Ahura, who makes the decisions;
“ It is whatever he wills, that will happen to us.”


" If that is so," said the Shaper of the Bovine, 


" Then, shall both my soul and that of the calving cow, urge Mazdah with questions,
“ And placate him with outstretched hands.


“ Praying that no destruction may affect the just-living farmer,
“ He, who dwells among the Drujists." 


In answer, Ahura Mazdah, who knows the decrees which make for wisdom, himself spoke:


“ In as much as neither overlord, nor rightly appointed judge exists for thee,
“ I, who am the fashioner, therefore, shaped thee for the farmer and pasturer." 


This decree, which provided fat food for the cattle, destined the cattle milk-food for the hungry farmer and pasturer. Uttered by Ahura Mazdah  in agreement with Asha, through his bountiful teaching; but the bovine pair were at a loss for someone to enforce this decree on earth. 


The bovine and calving cow, they asked: 


" What man hast thou, O’ Vohu Manah, who could tend us both among men?" 


Vohu Manah answered: 


" The only person known unto me here who has hearkened to our teaching is,
“ Zarathushtra Spitama;


“ He is desirous of proclaiming the divine thought, for Mazdah and Asha
“ So we will endow his words with attractive sweetness."


Thereupon, the Soul of the Bovine lamented: 


" Woe is me that it is I who must, for a preparer for my needs, 
“ Put up with the impotent speech of an impotent man! 
“ I, who wished for myself a self depending divine ruler 
“ In what age shall he, who may give me energetic help, arise”


Zarathushtra spoke:


“ I beg you, O’ Ahura, and Asha that you will give to these-two, 
“ The Soul of the Bovine creation, and the calving cow 
“ The vigour and ruling power, that gives peace of dwelling
“ Through Vohu Manah's assistance;


“ As for me, O’ Mazdah, I have in this, my call to serve thee, 
“ Recognized thee, as the original provider of supplies
“ Where else, except with thee, O’ Mazdah is Asha, Vohu Manah, and Xshathra? 


“ O’ mortals, receive me among you, that I may impart to you instruction,
“ For the great Magian cause.


“ Grant us help, now O’ Ahura Mazdah, 
“ For we intend to be of service to such divinities as you!” 


Song of Those With Regard


“ With Spenta Mainyu, and with the best Manah; with the Xshathra, and Armaiti, 
“ Ahura Mazdah gave Haurvatat and Ameretat,
“ For the deeds and speeches caused by Asha.


“ Mazdah is the father of Asha!” 


With this doctrinal thought Zarathushtra declared the best treasure of the most Spenta Mainyu, with utterances through the tongue, in the mouth of Vohu Manah, and works the deeds of Armaiti, with both his hands:


“ Thou art the bounteous father of this Mentality,
“ Who hast shaped the fortune-bringing cow, for this Zarathushtra,
“ And peace-bringing Armaiti, as her pasture,
“ When consulting with Vohu Manah,


“ O’ Mazdah! 


“ The Drujists have apostatised from this, bounteous father of the Mentality,
“ Namely thyself, O’ Mazdah, 


“ But, not thus the Ashaists!


“ For, though a man be no more than poor,
“ Yet should he, to the best of his ability, entertain the Ashaists,
“ Or, in addition,
“ If a man is powerful, he should effect evil for the Drujists.
“ And through that Spenta Mainyu, O’ Ahura Mazdah,
“ Mayest thou teach to the Ashaist what doctrines and deeds are the best.


“ But, shall the Drujist, who lacks thy good pleasure,
“ And dwells with the deeds of the Aka Manah, have a share of this best reward?


“ Mayest thou, O’ Ahura Mazdah, give that best through Spenta Mainyu
“ At a distribution to both contending parties by means of the good-fire,
“ And by the support of Armaiti and Asha?
“ I feel sure that this distribution of the best 
“ Will cause many who desire the best to choose conversion to it!”


The Steppe and the Catacomb


“ Thy Asha, or thy Vohu Manah?
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, whom may I, with certainty, invoke to protect my cattle and myself?
“ On what help may my soul count?


“ How, O’ Mazdah, might a man seek possession of a fortune-bringing cow, 
“ If he desire both her and the pasture?


“ By living justly among the many men who appreciate the comforts,
“ And agricultural benefits of the sun, 
“ By settling open-lands or badlands, yet belonging to the bad Drujists,
“ Land to be acquired and settled-down, as a clever man would do, cleverly; 
“ And, which may be given as gifts.


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, 
“ Through asha, shall legal right be the portion of this pioneer;
“ Getting possession of what settlements the good teaching, and teacher,
“ Zarathushtra taught him;


“ To take possession with wit, Vohu Manah, good management, and Xshathra;
“ Who, through the vigour of compensation, and thereby, paying an equivalent,
“ May acquire the nearest estate,
“ Which the nomadic Drujists may yet share for some time.”


These Settlers of the Nomads


“ When the wisher, for a recently settled home,
“ Stands upon the path leading to the above-mentioned open or badlands,
“ Then I, Zarathushtra, will hear these faithful home-seekers, going to their new dwelling;
“ Which, when occupied, will be full of grateful praise!


“ And I, Zarathushtra, praising you, O’ Ahura Mazdah with Asha, the best manah,
“ And Xshathra,  
“ I will, for this joyful information about new settlements, worship you; 
“ As, you also have rejoiced with, and for your prophet's joy.


“ O’ Mazdah Ahura,
“ I have, through Asha, aroused myself, on account of  your visible and manifest help,
“ Through hand sought labour which restores us, the faithful, to prosperous well-being,
“ From the consequent destitution of the furious rapine of the Drujists.


“ I, Zarathushtra, who through reverence, am the friend of Asha,
“ And who give directions to the settlements through my tongue,
“ I pray, O’ Mazdah, that the wisher-settler may also raise up his speech in a word 
“ As I do,
“ And with the aid of Vohu Manah, may he teach the commands of my understanding,
“ By thus, enlisting missionaries for you.


“ O’ Mazdah, with Asha and Vohu Manah,
“  I will yoke on speedy runners, thick-set and strong, with spurs of adoration of you,
“ Through which runners, may you and your cause drive on to progress,
“ And might you aid me too.


“ Last, but not least,


“ With these verses, which are famous as verses of zeal,
“ I will, with stretched out hands, encompass you, O ‘Mazdah,
“ You also, O’ Asha, with the reverence of the faithful,
“ You also, all ye divinities, with the full ability of Vohu Manah!”


Zarathushtra; Ever-the-Wanderer


“ O’ Mazdah, and Asha,
“ I will come towards you, praising with these hymns, and with the deeds of vohu manah;


“ When shall I rule, at will, over my compensation? 


“ Shall I, then, become recipient thereof, 
“ Caring for that of the well-disposed, the faithful believer?


“ Whatever deeds I may have done before,
“ And through Vohu Manah, whatever objects interest the eye,
“ Still, they are as nothing when compared to the lights of the sky,
“ The sun, the dawn, the shimmering bull of days!


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, and Asha, 
“ All these exist for your adoration 


“ I will preach that I am your praise-singer,
“ O’ Mazdah, O’ Asha, may the Creator of Life, through the help of Vohu Manah, prosper 


“ And may I be this, as far as I am able, and can
“ Whatever genuine work is most suitable to his will!”


Whom through Asha:


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ How should I pray, when I wish to pray to one like you,
“ O‘ Mazdah, may one like you, who is friendly, teach one like me, 
“ May you give us supporting aide through the friendly Asha,
“ And tell us how you may come to us, with Vohu Manah? 


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ Whether, at the beginning of the best life,
“ Are the retributions to be of profit to their recipients? 
“ And, whether he or I, who is bounteous to all through asha, 
“ And, who watches the end, through his mainyu,
“ Whether he is the life healing friend of the people?


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ Who was the first father of Asha by giving birth to him,
“ Who established the sun-lit days and the star glistering sphere and the Milky Way, 
“ Who, apart from thee, established the law by which the moon waxes and wanes?
“ These and other things, would I like to know! 
.
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ Who was, from beneath, sustaining the earth and the clouds,
“ So that they would not fall down? Who made the waters and the plants?
“ Who yoked the Two Swift Ones, Thunder and Lightning,
“ To the wind and to the clouds ?
“ Who is the creator of Vohu Manah?


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ Who produced well made lights and darkness,
“ Who produced sleep, well induced through laborious waking,
“ Who produced the dawns and the noon, through the contrast with the night,
“ Whose daily changes, act for the enlightened believers, as monitors of their interests?


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ Is the message I am about to proclaim genuine?
“ Does Armaiti support Asha through deeds?
“ Dost thou, with Vohu Manah, destine the xshathra for these believer,
“ For whom, but these believers, didst thou shape the fortune-bringing cattle? 


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ Who shaped prized armaiti with xshathra
“ Who, by guidance and education, rendered sons reverent to their fathers,
“ It is I, who strives to learn to recognize thee, through the spenta mainyu, 
“ As giver of all! 


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ I would like to know what sort of a purpose is thine,
“ That I may be mindful of it.
“ What are thy utterances, about which I asked?
“ Through the aid of vohu manah; the proper knowledge of life through asha,
“ How shall my soul, encouraged by bliss, arrive at that good reward? 


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ How may I accomplish the sanctification of those spirits,
“ To whom thou, the well-disposed master of the coming kingdom,
“ Xshathra, hast pronounced promises about its genuine blessings,
“ Promising that those spirits shall dwell,
“ In the same dwelling, with Asha and Vohu Manah?


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ How will such a promise properly nurture good deed,
“ Which are the best qualities of these, my followers' spirits,
“ Together with utterances of humility
“ How will the promise cause my followers' settlements to prosper through asha? 


“  O’ Mazdah, the aspirations of my mystic wisdom shall wish for thy second birth!


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee speak to me truly.
“ How will Armaiti actually, in deeds, extend over those persons,
“ To whom thy spirit was announced as a doctrine,
“ On account of whom I first was elected, and acknowledged, and whom I love?


“ All others I look upon with hostility of mentality!


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ How shall I, from among those whom I am addressing,
“ Tell the Ashaist from the Drujist?
“ To which of these two is the enemy of life opposed? 
“ Or shall I take it for granted that whoever opposes thy prophet
“ Is an enemy and a Drujist?


“ What is the real condition of affairs with him, the Drujist?
“ Shall he not be considered an enemy?


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly!
“ How may we drive off from us, the Drujist? 


“ I exclaim: 
“ Down with those, who being full of disobedience,
“ Do not strive to be together with Asha,
“ Nor desire to understand the problems of Vohu Manah."


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ How may I deliver the Druj into the hands of Asha,
“ That I may smite them down according to the promises,
“ Or, through the power of thy words of thy doctrines,
“ So that even within, or among the Drujists, I may accomplish powerful destruction,
“ That those torturers and haters may perish?


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly. 
“ Tell me whether, in order to protect me, thou hast taken control of this opposition?
” Whenever the two hostile armies shall meet,
“ By those commands of righteousness which thou shalt maintain,
“ To which, of the two parties, wilt thou give victory?


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ Tell me who comes victoriously with thy doctrines to protect those who are existent?
“ Do thou, who art life healing, clearly designate to me an established judge?
“ To him, whosoever he be, to whom thou wishest to give this appointment as judge,
“ To him may then Sraosha, with Vohu Manah, then come! 


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ How shall I carry out the object inspired by you,
“ Namely, my attachment to you, in order that my speech may grow mighty,
“ And that, by that word of mine the adherent of Asha,
“ May in the future commune with Haurvatat, and Ameretat?


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ How shall I earn through asha, that compensation which was announced to me,
“ On account of Haurvatat and Ameretat?
“ Namely, ten mares with stallions, and a camel?
“ So that I might in the future impart the mystic word of these twin divinities,
“ To those adherents of Asha.


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, this I ask of thee, speak to me truly.
“ How shall be punished, 
“ He who does not give the above compensation to me, who earned it,
“ To me who earned it by uttering just teachings?
“ I know what punishment will overtake him at the last day;
“  But tell me, what punishment will overtake him here in this first life?


“ O’ people, you might as well obey me, as I shall let you judge for yourselves:
“ Have you prospered under the rule of the Daevas? 
“ As umpires between us, I shall ask those for the sake of whose feeding,
“ The Karapas and the Uxic give up the cattle to Aeshma,
“ For whom the Kavays have made the cattle lament exceedingly,
“ Instead of through asha prospering pasturage, so as to care for the cattle.”


Song of Two Lives:


“ To the utmost of my ability, will I teach men to seek Asha,


“ O’ Mazdah,
“ With outstretched hands, and by reverent prayer for support, 
“ I will entreat, as the first blessing of the spenta mainyu
“ That all my actions, may be performed with the aid of Asha, 
“ That I may receive the understanding of Vohu Manah,
“ And that I may thus satisfy the soul of the bovine.


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, through Vohu Manah, this do I entreat you, 
“ To grant me both lives, that of the body and of the mind,
“ With the felicity, with which Mazdah, through Asha,
“ Supports those to whom Mazdah gives the two-lives, for their comfort.


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, 
“ I, who will sing hymns to you 
“ Through asha and vohu manah, as never before; 
“ I will also sing hymns to those faithful believers
“ For whom Armaiti prospers the never decreasing Realm-of-Xshathra.


“ Hither, O’ you divinities, come to my support; come to my call!
“ I, who with Vohu Manah, am mindful to watch over the soul of the bovine, 
“ Who knows with what compensations are rewarded 
“ For the deeds of the kind, inspired by Ahura Mazdah, 
“ To the utmost of my ability, will I teach men to seek Asha!”


Prayer for Help and Mercy


“ O’ Asha! 
“ When shall I, through thee, behold Vohu Manah, as an expert-knower,
“ Discovering or attaining, among the Magian tribe, the throne,
“ And the tribe-men's sraosha,
“ For the prospering of the cause of the most powerful, Mazdah Ahura
“ With this mystic word of promise?
 
“ I will cause those savages to choose the cause of the greatest, Mazdah Ahura!
 
“ O’ Mazdah Ahura, come with long life of asha gifts, with Vohu Manah, 
“ With just utterances; 
“ Give these to Zarathushtra as the means of vigorous support. 
“ Give these to us; that we, thereby, may overcome the hostilities of the enemy. 
“ O’ Asha, grant, this compensation:
“ Namely, the felicities of vohu manah;
“ Grant, O ‘Armaiti, the wish of Vishtaspa and of myself!
“ O’ thou greatest ruler, grant a ready hearing unto him, who prepares with the word
“ For the best, do I entreat the Lord Ahura, 
“ Like willed with thee, the best manah, and with the best ashas.


“ Supplicating Asha, for the hero Frashaoshtra, and me,
“ And for whomsoever thou wouldst grant asha, 
“ For all the age of Vohu Manah,


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, we would not vex you by those supplications,
“ Nor would we vex the best manah, because of these expected benefactions;


“ Rather, would we haste to offer praise to you, 
“ Who are the best prosperer of human wishes for profit, 
“ The Xshathra-realm to come


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, crown with attainments;
“ The desire of such clever persons, thou knowest, through Asha,
“ To be, both, worthy and of Vohu Manah!


“ This I pray, because I know that supplicatory words reach you, and are effective.


“ I, who am to protect the worship of Asha and Vohu Manah for ever, 
“ I beg thee, Mazdah Ahura to reveal to me the truth,
“ So that I may be able to proclaim: 
“ What is the development of the present, the first dispensation of life, out of thy Mainyu
“ As if it was being uttered through thy mouth.”


The Marriage Ceremony:


“ The best riches, that have been heard of, are those of Zarathushtra,
“ Since Ahura Mazdah grants to him by asha, for all eternity,
“ Felicities, a good life, and the conversion of those who deceived him;
“ And Zarathushtra prays that his followers may learn to formulate,
“ Correctly, the utterances and deeds of the good spirit-of-religion. 


“ May Vishtaspa, and Maidyomangh, the Zarathushtnan son of the Spitama-family,
“ And Frashaoshtra,
“ Establish the straightly, just, paths of the spirits for whom Ahura has appointed saviours
“ Who strive, through utterances and deeds inspired by Vohu Manah,
“ To utter cheerfully, hymns for the adoration of Mazdah, in order to give him satisfaction.


“ Pouruchista, daughter of Haechataspa,
“ Thou youngest of the daughters of Zarathushtra,
“ Zarathushtra gives to thee this Jamaspa, as a husband,
“ Who will impress on thee communion with Vohu Manah, Asha, and Mazdah;
“ So take counsel, with thy understanding, so that it may become most bounteous 
“ When it has become full of well disposed armaiti. 


The bride spoke: 


“ Him, will I emulate!” 


The bridegroom spoke: 


“ Her will I choose, who shall, generously, distribute service to father, husband,
“ Pasturer, gentleman, Ashaist and pagan!” 


 The bride spoke: 


“ The sunlit fruit of Vohu Manah shall be mine, I shall have a child! “


The bridegroom spoke:


“ May Ahura Mazdah grant it to thy good spirit for all eternity!”


Zarathushtra spoke: 


“ I utter admonitions to those who are being married, and to maidens;
“ That which I am counselling to you, you should heed, remember, and realize.
“ Through spiritual precepts, striving for the life of vohu manah,
“ Vying with each other in asha and spiritual precepts,
“ Thus shall you be happy.


“ Thus shall you be genuine men and women, husband and wife
“ For, can you discover any advantage in belonging to the party of the Drujists? 


“ What the Drujists desire shall be kept away from their bodies!


“ Ahura Mazdah shall present bad food to those, who then shall be crying woe,
“ And shall withdraw comfort from the law-scorning Druj;
“ Thereby, with these other contrasting actions, 
“ You destroy the spiritual life for yourselves.


“ But to you the newly-married pair shall be granted 
“ The compensation of the Magian cause 
“ That most hearty zeal be rooted in your body;
“ But if you dismiss, and abandon, the Magian cause,
“ You shall, at the last, cry woe
“ When the spirits of the Druj dodge downwards, and disappear.”


The Spreading Corruption: Place of Decay


“ Thereby, let all the malefactors be deluded through causes other than the Magian,
“ Through whose good rulers, peace shall be given to the dwellings and villages.
“ On the contrary, 
“ Let the malefactors through whom are effected, murder and bloody deed,
“ Let them all be abandoned, and cry upwards in vain.
“ Let the greatest torture attack those captives of destruction
“ And let that, too, occur soon! 


“ Vagabond nomads shall reach the Place of Decay, with the malefactors,
“ Who, being law-scorning, and as to their bodies, doomed,
“ Are seeking to degrade the worthy Magians.


“ Where is the Ashaist lord who will deprive them of freedom and of life,
“ And who shall establish the Xshathra, by which, O’ Mazdah,
“ Thou shalt give, to the poor man who lives justly, that better part?”


Prayer Through Struggle


“ Among the foremost of thy worshippers, we offer to thee,
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, through the mind of the good mentality,
“ All the deeds, speeches, and hymns;
“ Through which thou shalt bestow ameretat, asha, xshathra, and haurvatat,


“ And we bear the praises of thy grateful people with a bounteousness, 
“ Such as, we would gratefully follow along the paths of asha,
“ Even into the outer realms of the heavenly presence, of a divinity such as you.


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah and to Asha,
“ To thee we shall, with reverence, offer the oblation
