“ That you, with Vohu Manah, may cause all substantial beings which are in the Xshathra 
“ To mature into perfection, 
“ Seeing that the perfecting of the beneficent man is at all times valuable 
“ In the sight of one like you.


“ We wish, O’ Mazdah, that thy fire, whose strength is Asha,
“ May be a promised swift, powerful, clear, delightful help for supporting the land
“ Or the people. 
“ And, that it may be, for the enemies, a visible, suggestive, and practical token of harm.


“ Have you enough xshathra, have you enough wealth,
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, for the deeds which I urge you to do
“ Namely, with Asha and Vohu Manah, to protect your poor?


“ Through our preaching among the Daevic enemies, savages, and doomed-men,
“ We will declare you to all foreigners.


“ Since you therefore actually have the above powers, O’ Ahura Mazdah,
“ With Asha and Vohu Manah, grant me as token, thereof,
“ A change for better, now in this life; 
“ So that I may, the more, approach you, with greater joy and adoration.


“ O’ Lord, listen to me, who wishes to ask thee about certain people,
“ Are they faithful to thee?
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, though they know of Vohu Manah, 
“ They turn the difficulties of traditionally inherited doctrine
“ Unto their own advantage by sophistries!


“ For, I know none other but you, O’ Asha,
“ Therefore, protect us with such specious deeds, under which, lurk danger for many.
 
“ They are intimidating us, especially me, who am the weaker!


“ O’ Mazdah, Bendva is strong with hate of thy commands, 
“ Those who think not of Asha, they are surely distant from Vohu Manah!
“ Those evil doers who drive away Bounteous Armaiti,
“ So highly prized by the expert-knower.


“ O’ Mazdah, because they have no share of Vohu Manah,
“ They are shunned by the man endowed by Asha,
“ As much as the savages are shunned by us.


“ The clever expert-knower will say he holds to the deeds of Vohu Manah,
“ And to the bounteous, Creator Armaiti, the associate of Asha,
“ And to all thy hopes, that are to be realized in thy Xshathra, O’ Ahura Mazdah.


“ Thus, for that expert-knower
“ Both Haurvatat and Ameretat serve, for food, 
“ And Armaiti, ever, has caused the two enduring and vital divinities to grow
“ Through the Xshathra of Vohu Manah and through Asha;
“ Thus, O’ Mazdah, thou blessest the opponents of thy enemies.


“ What is thy decree? What is thy wish? 
“ What praise, what hymn, wouldst thou have me raise to thee?


“ In order that we may hear it, do thou, O’ Ahura Mazdah,
“ Reveal what compensation thou wilt grant as a reward for keeping thy observances?


“ Teach us, O’ Asha, the passable paths of Vohu Manah, 
“ The way of Vohu Manah, of which thou toldst me,
“ That which is well formed by Asha, on which the spirits of the saviours progress,
“ Which thou, O’ Mazdah, hast assigned for the clever,
“ As one of the compensations which are at thy disposal.”


For Those Who Labour


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, which choice compensation, 
“ Given to my corporeal life, through the action of Vohu Manah,
“ To whomsoever belongs to the groups of those who labour at herding calving cows?


“ For, your compensations consist of good wisdom of the understanding,
“ Which causes prosperity for the caste of labouring men.


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, with Vohu Manah, and with Asha,
“ Inform me of the best teachings and deeds and speeches, 
“ And, what praise of ours you claim as your due.
“ Through Xsthathra grant that, according to your will, humanity may be progressive.


Prayer for Understanding


“ Minding these, your commands, O’ gods, let us pronounce speeches!


“ They are unheeded by those who would, by the commands of Druj,
“ Destroy the substance of Asha,
“ But they are most acceptable to them who will trust in Mazdah.


“ But, since the preferable path is not always obvious;
“ As heaven appointed arbiter and judge over both parties, 
“ Will I go to you, that we may live in accordance with Asha? 


“ In order that I may cause all men to choose aright, 
“ I pray thee, O’ Ahura Mazdah, with the tongue of thy mouth,
“ To tell what satisfaction, taught through asha, thou wilt give to both contending parties, 
“ Through mentality and fire;
“ What is thy command for the enlightened?
“ That we may understand that command.


“ And this command is, 
“ That as far as Asha and the other Ahuras Mazdah are willing to be invoked,
“ Through ashay, armaiti, and the best manah,
“ So far shall I seek for myself the xshathra by the vigorous increase
“ Whereof, we may defeat the Druj.


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, tell me what is not to occur, and what is to occur
“ In order that I may distinguish that,
“ And whatever success, therein, you may have given me through asha,
“ As the better thing,
“ In order that I may understand it through vohu manah 
“ And may ponder over this so that by understanding it fully,
“ I may gather, therefrom, a reward.”


Truth in Justice:


“ This is the best reward of life, namely, that xshathra,
“ Which the man who receives it, may for his own concurrent advantage, 
“ Increase for Mazdah, through vohu manah.


“ May this best reward be granted to him, who after having discovered for himself,
“ The right solution to these following problems that distress me,


" Tell me, sincerely, Asha 's mystic word, which is the secret of Haurvatat and Ameretat,
“ Whether Mazdah was the first one to fill the glories of heaven
“ With lights of stars, or flames? 


“ Whether Mazdah, through understanding, created asha,
“ And whether asha will maintain the best activity of vohu manah.


“ Whether Mazdah shall cause these, Asha and Vohu Manah,
“ To prosper through the holy mentality, which is ever the same until the present time.“


When, Zarathushtra, with his own eye, by looking at nature, and through his mind, by puzzling out its significance, comprehended Mazdah and Manah. Then, Zarathushtra understood that Mazdah was both the first and youngest of creation and the father of Vohu Manah, the genuine creator of asha and the ruling lord in the deeds of life: 


“ That, O’ Mazdah Ahura, to thee belongs both Armaiti and the Shaper of the Bovine
“ Which was part of the understanding of the holy mentality. 
“ That when, for the cow, thou didst ordain a path of freedom of will,
“ Following which, she might repudiate the herdsman, and go to abide with the nomad.
“ Then, she chose for herself from among the two possible lords,
“ The herdsman or nomad,
“ The herdsman who would follow her, namely, the Ashaist,
“ Who feels that it is his mission to see to it 
“ That all things that belong to Vohu Manah prosper, 
“ And, who in return, is prospered by him,
        


“ Whereas, 
“ The nomad shall not get a share of Vohu Manah’s favourable report at the judgement, 
“ Even though he should urge for it,
 “ So long as he will not herd cattle.


“ That at first thou, O’ Mazdah, with thy mind and understanding, 
“ Thou didst shape substance and spirits, and didst establish body and life,
“ And deeds, and doctrines;
“ Whereby, men who exercised their faculties of choice might develop convictions.”


All-Seerer:


“ Amidst such sublime, issues vulgar men, who speak either falsely or justly, 
“ The expert-knower or the ignorant. 
“ Each, according to the fancy of his heart and mind, dares to raise his impudent voice,
“ Where Armaiti counsels successively with the spirits who yet are wavering.


“ O’ Mazdah, thou with Asha, keepest a watch,
“ With thy watchful gleaming eyes, over all these men who ask questions
“ Openly or furtively;
“ And over all officials who inflict the greatest penance for even a small transgression. 


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, I ask thee about these conditions, present and future
“ Namely, what compensations will be given to satisfy the claims of the Ashaist?
“ And what compensations shall be enforced from the Drujist?
“ How shall both stand at the time of awarding the compensations?


“ O’ Ahura, I ask thee what shall be the punishments
“ Of those who encourage the dominion of the Drujist?
“ Of those who, cannot make their living without violence to cattle 
“ And to men devoted to herding them? 


“ O’ Mazdah Ahura, I ask thee whether the well disposed man,
“ Who may strive to improve the houses, the villages, the clans and the provinces,
“ Through Asha, 
“ Whether he may at all become a being like unto thee?
“  If so, when shall he arise unto this likeness,
“ And what deeds he shall do to become such?


“ Tell me O’ lord,
“ Which is the more important object of choice,
“ That of the Ashaist or that of the Drujist?
“ Do thou, who art the expert-knower, inform me who would become one?
“ And do not permit the ignorant man to continue deluding,
“ Such as me, who would like to learn


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, be thou to us an instructor of Vohu Manah!”


Cleansing of Flame:


“ O’ well disposed believer,
“ Hearken not to the mystic-words or teachings of any of the Drujists.
“ For these would reduce house, village, clan or province, to misfortune or death;
“ Rather oppose them with the weapon! 


“ But, hearken to him who thought out Asha. 
“ That is, Zarathushtra, who is the life healing expert-knower;


“ Him, who is able to vindicate his tongue's speeches at will, 
“ At the latter day when, O’ Ahura Mazdah, with thy red fire,
“ The good compensations of the two contending parties are to be distributed; 
“ With thy red fire, produced by the two good rubbing sticks,
“ Destruction, lasting darkness, bad food, and imprecations, 
“ Shall, at the latter day, be kept away from whomsoever
“ Identifies himself with the Ashaists; 


“ Beware, O’ Drujists, it is to that sort of existence that your evil spirit may lead you! 


“ From the resources of his innate glory,
“ Ahura Mazdah shall grant sustained communion and fullness
“ Of Haurvatat, and Ameretat, and of Asha, of Xshathra, and Vohu Manah;
“ To whomsoever is a friend to Ahura Mazdah, in mind and deeds.


“ The man who is well-disposed, understands this as clearly as does Mazdah,
“ Who knows, with the divine manah, 
“ It is this well disposed man who holds asha in union with the good xshathra,
“ Through his speech and deeds; 
“ It is this well disposed man who is the most prospering companion to thee,
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah.”


Call to Pasture:


“ In accordance with these laws of the first life
“ The judge will enact, the most just decisions for the Drujist,
“ As well as the Ashaist,
“ And, for the man who combines the false and just actions,
“ And does whatever may suit the needs of the false and the just.


“ But whoever, by speech, word, or deed, do harm to the Drujist,
“ Or converts one to the good or instructs a fellow believer,
“ They are those, who are thereby, working for the divine will,
“ And for the pleasure of Ahura Mazdah in the great work of the preparation.


“ Whoever, through being a gentleman by birth,
“ Is best inclined to the Ashaist, or which herd-labourer, or an expert peer,
“ Or is zealous for the cattle,
“ O’ Ahura, 
“ Surely he will come to be on the pasturage of Asha and Vohu Manah.”


Sraosha:


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, I, who by praying, would,
“ Conjure away disobedience and badness from what the opponents think of thee,
“ And, contrariness from the gentleman and the neighbourhood of the Drujist,
“ From the group of labouring men, and cursing from the peer,
“ And, poor farmers fodder, from the pasturage of the cattle; 


“ I, who, will invoke Sraosha, as the greatest of all divinities, for help to man;
“ Having reached long life in the realm of Vohu Manah and the Asha straightened paths,
“ On which Ahura Mazdah dwells;


“ I, who, as priest coming, through asha, from the best mind,
“ Desire to walk the just paths of the bounteous mentality
“ Going from us, the Ashaist's priests, towards the pastures 
“ Which, advisory managers, ought to work through the vohu manah.  
“  O’ Ahura Mazdah, for these two objects, I wish to consult together,
“ Both the best mind and the bounteous mentality, visibly!


“ Come, hither to me, O’ you best divinities; come here personally, O’ Mazdah,
“ Visibly, with Asha and Vohu Manah!
“ Inform me how I may be heard before or outside of the Magians;
“ And, for this purpose, let reverent services of worship be performed,
“ Clearly and manifestly among us.”


Prayer for Good Life


“ O’, do thou regard the interests which I am advancing, through vohu manah,
“ The hymn, O’ Mazdah, addressed to one like you 
“ And my grateful communions, with asha?


“ Give me your twin enduring possessions of Ameretat and Haurvatat!


“ But let me, O’ Mazdah, 
“ Bring the holy mentality of these two divine companions,
“ Who prosper the Ashaist with just life,
“ Unto the comfortable dwellings,
“ With me, who have the best manah, 
“ In thus, arousing the support of these two divinities,
“ Whose souls are accompanying each other,


“ O’ Mazdah, distribute, from thy delight, all the pleasures of life,
“ Which were, and are, and are yet becoming;
“ Cause my body to grow as I wish, with vohu manah, xshathra, and asha.


“ Thou Asha, who art the strongest Ahura of Mazdah and Armaiti,
“ Prospering, as if they were earthly substance, Asha and Vohu Manah,
“ Xshathra, hear me, pity me. 


“ When, to every man, shall retribution come? 


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, arise up to me, grant me, through Armaiti, vitality.
“ Through the most bounteous mentality, grant me strength.
“ Through good ada, and through asha, grant me powerful might,
“ And through vohu manah, grant compensation.


“ In order to grant me support,
“ O’ thou wide glancing divinity, O’ Ahura, O’ Bounteous Armaiti,
“ Show me that of the incomparable riches of thy realm,
“ The compensations of Vohu Manah;
“  Instruct the spirits through asha!


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah,
“ Zarathushtra offers as an oblation, his own body, through Asha
“ And the first fruits of Vohu Manah, the sraosha and xshathra of his deeds
“ And uttered words.”


The Flame of Ambition


“ Success to me, to you, and to whosoever will,  
“ May Ahura Mazdah, who rules at will over all things,
“ Grant that both enduringness and vitality may come to thee, O’ believer,


“ Of thee, O’ Lord, do I desire this!


“ As for me; grant me, that I maintain my hold on asha,
“ Do thou, O’ Armaiti, grant me riches, compensations, and the life of vohu manah?
“ Ye, Vohu Manah will give the best of all things to this Zarathushtra;
“ According to his wish,
“ He will give him the comfort of heaven.


“ O’ Mazdah, through thy Spenta Mainyu
“ Reveal all these blissful mysteries of Vohu Manah 
“ Which thou givest, through asha, with all the joys that are long and vital, on every day.


“ May he, who, like thee, O’ Mazdah, is faithful, well-informed, and bounteous,
“ Who would teach us the just paths of profit,
“ Namely, those of the good life, both corporeal and mental,
“ That lead to the real worlds where dwells Ahura,
“ May he arrive to what is better than good.


“ In order that I may receive the power of vohu manah,
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, I would, in thee, acknowledge heroism and bounteousness.
“ Through the hand in which thou holdest the compensation to be given
“ To Drujist and Ashaist,
“ And through the glow of thy judgement, fire, whose vigour is asha. 


VISION I. Discrimination:


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, I acknowledged thee, as the bounteous divinity when, 
“ At the beginning, I beheld thee at the birth of life 
“ When thou didst establish proper compensations for deeds and words:


“ A bad compensation for the bad, and a good compensation for the good;
“ Which is to occur, with thy skill, at the last crisis of of creation;


“ At which crisis come thou, O’ Mazdah, with thy Spenta Mainyu,
“ With Xshathra, Vohu Manah, and Asha,
“ Through whose deeds the human-estates are prospered,
“ For which Armaiti formulates judgement of Mazdah's understanding 
“ That, which no one ever deceives.“


VISION II. About Thy Substance and Thyself:


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, I recognized thee, as the bounteous divinity,
“ Through the answer, with which I was inspired;
“ When, with vohu manah, some man came around to me, and asked:  


““ Who art thou? To what divinity belongest thou?
““ With what divine token wilt thou appoint the day for questioning,
““ About thy substance and thyself?””


“ Therefore I, Zarathushtra, said to him at first:


" I would wish to be a genuine enemy to the Drujist; a vigorous support for the Ashaist,
“ So that I may plan for the developments of the now limited xshathra,
“ With as much zeal as I am now praising and lauding thee, O’ Mazdah!”


VISION III. Theophany of Asha


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah,  I recognized thee, as the bounteous divinity
“ When, with vohu manah, some man came around to me, and asked a question: 


“ To this question,


 “" What cause dost thou intend to adopt?"”


“ I, Zarathushtra, answered:


" At each oblation of reverence brought to thy sacrificial fire
“ O’ Lord, I will, to the extent of my ability, fix my mind on asha 
“ O’ God, show me the asha whom I invoke!" 


“ You spoke: 


" In company with Armaiti and Asha, I have come,
“ For thy question was as the question of powerful men,
“ When some ruler insists on having his mighty powerful wish granted by thee,


“ Now ask us what thou hast to ask us!"


VISION IV.  Trouble Among Men


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, I recognized thee, as the bounteous divinity
“ When I was first instructed in your utterances;
“ And when with vohu manah, some man came around to me and asked:


“" Will the doing of that which you have told me is the best to do
“” Arouse trouble among men for me?"


“ And at the same time, when thou toldst me to answer him: 


“" Depend on Asha for support"”


“ Then to me, Zarathushtra, who was not disobedient,
“ Thou utteredst the command: 


“" Thou hadst better arouse thy prophecy before the coming of Sraosha,
““ Together with the rich Ashay, 
“” Who will distribute to both contending parties, compensations for their profit.””


VISION V. Seekers of the Realm


“ I recognized thee, O’ Ahura Mazdah, as the bounteous divinity when,
“ With vohu manah, some man came around to learn the details of my desire:


“" Give me the inalienable gift of long enjoyment of the desired existence
“” Which is said to be in thy xshathra.
““ Just as a wealthy man, according to his knowledge or ability, gives to a friend,
“” So that I might stand forth with all those who remember thy mystic word
““ And arouse those who may become church organizers of thy teachings.””


VISION VI. The Sower Reaps; Reward for Deeds


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, I recognized thee, as the bounteous
“ When, with vohu manah, some man came around to ask me a question.
“ Then, the best Tushnamatay inspired me to proclaim to him: 


" A man as important as an Ashaist should not curry favour with a Drujist,
“ For it is the duty of the Ashaist to consider all Drujists as hostile." 


“ Yea, O’ Ahura Mazdah, in as much as, Zarathushtra chooses the Mainyu of Mazdah, 
“ He teaches that each most bounteous should, to himself, attach Asha;
” By living in a vigorous corporeal manner.


” May armaiti be found in the xshathra that ever beholds the sun.
” Where we pray, may Armaiti, with Vohu Manah, grant compensation to all
” Through the instrumentality of the deeds they may have performed!”


Thing Well Remembered:


“ But thus, O’ souls desirous of hearing, 
“ I will utter those things worthy to be remembered by the expert-knower,
“ The praises for Ahura, and hymns worthy of Vohu Manah,
“ And things well remembered with the aid of Asha,
“ And the propitious omens beheld through the lights of the stars, 
“ Or of the altar-flames.


“ Listen with your ears to the best information; 
“ Behold with your sight, and with your mind; 
“ Man by man, each for his own person, 
“ Distinguish between both confessions, before this great crisis;


“ Consider again!”


The Two Hands:


“ At the beginning both these mentalities became conscious of each other.
“ The one being a mentality better in thought, and word, and deed, 
“ The other mentality was bad.


“ Now let the just man discriminate between these two,
“ And choose the benevolent one, 
“ Not the bad one.


“ When the twin-mentalities came together, 
“ They produced the first life, and lifelessness,
“ And settled on the state of the last condition of existence. 


“ The worst for the Drujists, but for the Ashaists the best mind.


“ The Drujist chose between these twin-mentalities,
“ The one who perpetrated the worst deeds.


“ But he, who was inspired by the most bountiful mentality,
“ That is clothed upon by the most adamantine-stone quarried heavens, as a garment,
“ And he, who cheerfully satisfied Ahura Mazdah with sincere deeds,
“ Chose asha!


“ The Daevaists did not discriminate accurately between these two.
“ Just as they were deliberating, there came upon them a delusion ,
“ So that they should choose the worst mind;
“ So that, all together, they rushed over to Aeshma
“ Through which they afflict the life of man with disease. 


“ And to this man, now sick, came Mazdah Ahura
“ With the Xshathra-realm, with Vohu Manah and with Asha.
“ Armaiti endowed the sick body of man with firmness and endurance,
“ So that he may become the first of those,
“ Surviving the tests of passing through the metal and through adanas.


“ And thereupon, when the punishments of those malefactors shall occur,
“ O’ Mazdah, then, the saved man shall obtain for thee, with the help of vohu manah,
“ The Xshathra-realm,
“ Which will be the fulfillment of the world's destiny.
“ And this will be obtained by those who shall deliver the Druj
“ Into the two hands of Asha.”


To Culminate Wisdom:


“ May we be those who shall make life progressive and purposeful.
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, assemble together, along with Asha, and come hither,
“ So that here where our thoughts formerly developed separately, 
“ They may now mature together, and fuse. 


“ Culminate and become wisdom!


“ Then shall the success of the Druj break down, 
“ And all those who shall be attaining a good renown, shall obtain their reward;
“ Meeting at the good dwelling of Vohu Manah, Mazdah, and Asha.


“ O’ you mortals, when, you have familiarized yourselves
“ With these commandments of Mazdah,
“ Which mean prosperity as opposed to adversity,
“ And the length of the suffering of the Drujists, 
“ as contrasted with the useful progress of the Ashaists; 


“ I repeat;
“ When you have fully realized the significance of this contrast, 
“ I feel quite sure none of you all, will hesitate or delay to enter
“ Into the desired abode of praise.”


Mayest Thou Ask:


“ Asha will procure the good Xshathra,
“ Which is a choice and most productive destiny,
“ For those who, with zeal, through their deeds, practice the best doctrines.


“ O’ Mazdah, 
“Grant, that I may effect, carry into execution and realize that realm!


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, first of all, assure me the xshathra of compensation,
“ And then thine, O’ Asha! 
“ And thine, O Armaiti! 
“ Through Vohu Manah!


“ O’, do you grant profit to the worshipper of you?
“ O’ Mazdah, thou art the first teacher of those, who with their deeds,
“ And in their tongue, commune with the utterances of Vohu Manah;
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, O’ Asha, 
“ May they attract the attention of your ears.


“ Well mayest thou ask, all these following questions:


“ Where are the compensations for griefs? 
” Where may we find pity?
” Where may men attain Asha?
” Where may a man establish the bounteous armaiti?
” Where may he establish the best manah?
” Where, O’ Mazdah, may he establish thy Xshathra?
“ Where may the pasturer, having become a just man through his deeds, acquire cattle?
“ Will he succeed in doing so, if being of good understanding?


“ He reverently prays to him who is a just judge,
“ Ruling over both compensations, for the benefit of the clever believers.


“ Ahura Mazdah is he, who at the last crisis of life, through his Xshathra,
“ Distributes what is better than good to any one who prepares the world for his will.


“ But, distributes what is worse than bad to everyone 
“ Who does not distribute contributions to Zarathushtra.


“ Mazdah, thou who art the Fashioner of the Bovine Creation,
“ The waters and the plants,
“ Through the most spenta mainyu, grant me ameretat and haurvatat 
“ Which are full of vitality, and are, through Vohu Manah,
“ Enduring in the holy doctrines.”


Bridge of Sifting:


“ O’ Mazdah, I shall speak for thee, about what is threatened against the Drujist,
“ What is wished for, and promised, to him who maintains Asha
“ I shall speak thus because it is proper to speak to the intelligent man,
“ Referred to as the expert-knower;
“ And besides, the prophet rejoices through the word which is told to the expert-knower. 


“ O’ Mazdah, and do thou, within the nature of both contending parties,
“ Set satisfaction, as a token of the accuracy of my prophecy of the judgement
“ By the metal, molten, through thy red fire, 
“ Which, as a sifter, shall harm the Drujist, but profit the Ashaist.


“ Wherefore, I am fearless; 
“ Even those who would kill me here are only bringing me closer to my reward,
“ O’ Mazdah, whereas; 
“ He, who would wish to kill me, not considering this coming event, 
“ He punishes himself by becoming malicious towards the creatures that are existent;
“ And, thereby, becomes a son of the Druj and will therefore share their fate,
“ While I, even though killed, will, for myself, call Asha, that he may come
“ With good compensation.


“ O’ Mazdah, such being the fate of my enemies, I would like to know 
“ Who is a friend to Spitama Zarathushtra?
“ Is it not he who has consulted with Asha? 
“ What is Bounteous Armaiti?
“ Who, as a just supporter for the Magian cause,
“ Showed himself to be considerate of Vohu Manah?


“ Returning to my enemies, not very much did he!


“ Kik!
“ That paederast, that vulgar fellow of the Kavay-tribe, ingratiated himself with me!
“ When at the bridge-passage of the earth,
“ He refused hospitality to Zarathushtra Spitama, and to both the oxen,
“ Whom he was driving along, shivering with cold.


“ Thus the spirit of the Druj destroys the genuine reward of the straight path;
“  And his soul trembles at the Bridge of the Sifting, which will make manifest his deeds,
“ Giving access to the other world.


“ Through his deeds, and through those evil words of their tongue, 
“ The Druj have perished from the path of Asha.


“ Even the friendly disposed Karapans
“ Do not cultivate their pasture properly by the proper rules.
“ They effect, by their deeds and doctrines, griefs for the bovine.
“ Doctrine will at last bring the Karapans into the Dwelling of the Druj! 


“ On the contrary, when Ahura Mazdah shall, as first comer, come into Garodman, 
“ The Dwelling of Praise,
“ I, expect, through having produced profits for Asha, and through Vohu Manah; 
“ The compensation which I taught to the Magians.”


Heroes and Saints:


“ Kava Vishtaspa has attained more than these, both rewards;
“ Together with the rule over the Magian tribe,
“ Through Asha as advisory manager, and through the verses of Vohu Manah,
“ He attained chisti; 
“ Thus, for us, the faithful, is Ahura Mazdah bounteous at wish.


“ Frashaoshtra Hvogva has shown and promised to me,
“ As wife, his daughter's prized body,
“ Now, in-order-that I may attain, to be the recipient of asha,
“ May the ruling Ahura Mazdah, grant to me,
“ Her desired body for the benefit of her good spirit
“ That she may be saved, through me as husband! 


“ May Jamaspa Hvogva, glorious with riches,
 “ Choosing this chisti, daughter of Zarathushtra,
“ Whom he marries in yasna through asha,
“ Also partake in that xshathra of vohu manah; 


“ May Ahura Mazdah grant to me, that they may find support with thee 
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah!


“ O’ Maidyomangha Spitama!
“ Zarathushtra, has given that prize, his daughter Sophia, to this Jamaspa,
“ Who, as having known through his spirit the first life, is wishing again the the second,
“  And says that thou hast the better part of life, 
“ Having practiced the decrees of Mazdah through thy deeds.


“ That better part, which is a profit of yours, of all you like willed four leaders, 
“ Vishtaspa, Frashaoshtra, Jamaspa, and Maidyomangha,
“ I repeat, that better part induces you to give utterance too
“ Utterances with which Armaiti, with Vohu Manah, being worshipped with reverence,
“ Will grant asha as a support of Mazdah.


“ I desire a good compensation such as this
“ To grow bounteous through the influence of Armaiti;
“ Such a person is prospering Asha with his doctrinal thought,
“ Chisti, through his utterances, deeds, and spirit; 
“ To such a person will, Ahura Mazdah, through vohu manah, grant the xshathra. 


“ If Mazdah Ahura knows, among any of the men who were, and who yet are living,
“ Any person, to whom, because of their hymns,
“ The best reward from asha is yet to come,
“ These men, like the above mentioned four heroes,
“ Even by their names will I worship publicly and individually;
“ And into their presence will I enter with praise!” 


The Gift of Love


“ All three classes, the kinsman lord, the worker, and the priestly peer, shall;
“ O’ Daeva, in spite of you,
“ Pray for the joys of Ahura Mazdah according to my conceptions,” 


Said Zoroaster.


The three classes answered: 


“ May we be thy messengers, to repel those who hostilely deceive you, 
“ O’ divine beings.”


To them replied Ahura Mazdah, who was in communion with Vohu Manah by Xshathra, with the gloriously sunny, well befriending Asha:
 
" We have accepted this, your holy, good, submissive confession 
“ Which we name Armaiti. She shall be ours."


Grehma & the Daeva-Darlings,


” You O’ Daevas, are all as a seed; descended from the bad mind,
“ And whatever mortal man will dare to reverence you, 
“ He shall be considered as belonging to the Druj,
“ For he is proud the opposite of Armaiti. 


“ You have become notorious, even to the seventh region of the earth.
“ Long since, preceded by the evil reputation of your deeds.
“ It is due to you, the worst behaving men are called daeva darlings
“ And are excluded from vohu manah, and fellowship in the congregation,
“ Perishing away from asha, and from the understanding of Mazdah Ahura.


“ Therefore, you will defraud men of good life here, and immortality beyond, 
“ Because with evil mind and bad speech, he, 
“ Grehma, of the evil mentality, advises the deeds,
“ With which he causes you, who are Daevas, and the Drujist to perish.


“ Grehma has so far succeeded in perpetrating the many violences 
“ Through which he has become notorious;  
“ But whether he shall continue this success, here on earth,
“ O’ Ahura, thou alone knowest, through thy Vohu Manah.


" But, of this I am sure, that in thy Xshathra, O’ Mazdah,
“ Your doctrinal decision shall be given for asha.
“ The expert-knower is not to commit any of these deeds of violence,
“ Whose fatal end, thou, O’ Ahura Mazdah, best knowest.


“ He is not to commit any of them, even if tempted to do so,
“ Through a well intentioned, misguided, desire for proper gain.
“ For it was such a desire, that led Grehma to become notorious
“ Through his glittering sword of violence.


“ As is well known, there was among these committers of violence 
“ A certain Yima, son of Vivahvant, 
“ Who in order to satisfy our men, gave pieces of beef to be devoured. 


“ I certainly expect to be divided from these sinners,
“ In thy discriminating-judgement, O’ Mazdah!


“ The Prophet of Gvil, Grehma, with his pronouncements will destroy the words,
“ That which form the understanding of life,
“ By hindering my wealth, the prized possession of vohu manah.
“ With these uttered expressions of my thought as a complaint, 
“ I appeal to thee, O’ Mazdah, through asha.


“ It is Grehma who destroys the effect of my words,
“ And who preaches that, the cattle and the sun are the worst objects to behold,
“ It is he who makes Drujists out of clever believers,
“ Who destroys the cultivated lands,
“ And raises the weapon against the Ashaist.


“ His followers would destroy my life.
“ They have had many consultations with the Drujists,
“ So as to deprive the Ashaist masters, and the mistresses,
“ Of the possession of their inheritance,
“ And to cause the Ashaists to apostatize from the best mind, O’ Mazdah.
“ And by their speeches, cause men to apostatize from their best deeds. 


Mazdah spoke:


" Bad are ye who destroy the life of cattle with cries of joy,
“ And who, to Asha, prefer Grehma, Karpa, and the power favourable to the Druj
“ Through which xshathra of the above Asha,
“ Grehma will be degraded to hell, the dwelling of the Worst Mind.
“ Where dwell the destroyers of this life, and then 


“” O’ Mazdah,””


“ He will complain!
“ Being moved by a desire for the message of thy prophet,
“ Who then, however, will keep him from beholding Asha.


“ Grehma plans the subjection of this Asha;
“ Long since he supports both the Kavayas and the powerful Drujists
“ Through his plans, which raises help unto the Drujist
“ And he cries that the cow is to be killed;
“ He will burn the Ashaist who repels death from her, by being of help to her.” 
Desolation:


“ But through these Ashaists, 
“ I will expel both the Karapans and Kavayites,
“ Who will not permit the Ashaists to rule their lives as they wish,
“ By the divine law of cattle herding.


“ These Ashaists, I pray will be borne to heaven,
“ The dwelling of Vohu Manah by the twins Haurvatat and Ameretat.
“ All this is from that best divinity, who is teaching in the wide light of the stars,
“ Or the altar-flame.”


“ O’ Mazdah Ahura, 
“ Thou controllest whomsoever threatens me with destruction, 
“ So that I may encourage the beloved believers, 
“ By setting limits to the violence of the Druj, 
“ By the words of my mouth!”


The Gathered Flock:


“ Now, will I speak out, 
“  Listen and hear! 


“ You, 
“ Who from far and near, have come to seek my word;


“ Now I exhort you clearly, to impress on your memory,
“ The evil teacher and his faults; 
“ For no longer shall the evil teacher,
“ Druj that he is,
“ Destroy the second life in the speech of his tongue;
“ Misleading to the evil life! 


The Opposition:


“ Now will I speak out!


“ At the beginning of life,
“ The holier mentality said to the opposing mentality, 
“ Who was more hostile:


“" Neither our thoughts, doctrines, plans, beliefs,
“” Utterances, deeds, individualities, nor souls agree!"”


The Good Spirit Teaches:


“ Now will I speak out! 


“ Of that,
“ Which at the beginning of life,
“ The knowing Ahura Mazdah said :


"” Those who do not practice the word, as I consider and declare it,
“” They shall have woe at the end of life!””


“ Now will I speak out!


“ What is the best of life?


“ Through Asha, O’ Mazdah,
“ Have I discovered thee, who hast created him,
“ Asha;


“ That, Mazdah is the father of the working Vohu Manah;


“ And that Armaiti, who produces good deeds, is his daughter;
“ And that the all detecting Ahura is not to be deceived. 


“ Now will I, who am the utterer of this word,
“ Which is the best for mortal men to hear,
“ Speak out what the most bounteous Ahura Mazdah said to me:


" Those, who for the attainment of this mystic mantric word,
“ Grant me their obedience, 
“ That they shall come up with Haurvatat and Ameretat, 
“ With the deeds of the good mainyu."


The Faithful Make Way For The Herdsmen: 


“ Now will I speak out about the greatest being of all,
“ Through Asha,


“ Praising Mazdah, who blesses all existent beings!
“ Let Ahura Mazdah hear, through Spenta Mainyu,
“ The fact that I consulted Vohu Manah as to how I should adore Mazdah,
“ Through whose understanding, may he, Vohu Manah, teach me the best teachings .


“ Let the preparers for the Conversion of the World,
“ Both, those who were,
“ And those who are yet becoming,
“ Wish for the profits of the compensations.


“ The successful soul of the Ashaist, 
“ Abiding in Ameretat, with enduringness,
“ While the Drujists shall endure griefs.
“ And all this Ahura Mazdah creates through Xshathra,
“ The coming of the kingdom,


“ Thou shalt seek to win Mazdah,
“ With such praises of reverence, as for instance this psalm:


" With my own eyes shall I now behold
“ The Heaven of the Good Mentality of Word and Deed;
“ Having, through Asha, known Ahura Mazdah, whom let us in heaven,
“ Set down adorations, for the filling of the dwelling of praise,
“ Garodman,


“ Him, along with Vohu Manah, shalt thou seek to satisfy, for us, 
“ Because it is he who, by his will, makes our fortune or misfortune,
“ May Ahura Mazdah through his xshathra grant, for the group of herdsmen,
“ Prospering of our cattle and men, by the proficiency of Vohu Manah, through Asha.” 


“ How shalt thou, O’ individual believer, with hymns of Armaiti,
“ Magnify him who is reputed to be Ahura Mazdah for eternity?


“ Since through Asha, and Vohu Manah, he has promised us that in his Xshathra,
“ We shall obtain haurvatat and ameretat;
“ But, 
“ We shall obtain that, his heavenly dwelling, through vitality and enduringness.“


Scorn:


“ Whoever therefore, in the future,
“ Scorns the Daevas, and the men who scorn him, Zarathushtra,
“ And all other luke warm neutrals, 
“ Whoever is devoted to him, Zarathushtra,
“ Shall be considered, by the bounteous individuality of Zarathushtra,
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, who is saviour and  master of the house, 
“ As his friend, brother, or father.”


The Converts Turned Away:


“ Ever has Bendva hindered me, and proved himself the greater of us two
“ O’ Mazdah.


“ When I, through asha, was trying to satisfy the misled crowds,
“ O’ Mindful Mazdah, came to my support with thy good ada,
“ And with the vohu manah,
“ Through whom thou foreknowest the impending destruction of that Bendva.


“ But that Bendva's teacher, Grehma,
“ That apostate from Asha, that Druj, has long hindered me.
“ He fails to insist on having with him the Bounteous Armaiti,
“ O’ Mazdah, neither does he counsel with Vohu Manah.


“ In our belief,  asha is set down as the means to profit;
“ While,on the contrary, in their teaching,
“ The lie of the Druj is set down as the means to harm.
“ Consequently, I zealously insist on communion with Vohu Manah,
“ And on swearing off, renouncing all association with the Druj,
“ Who, through perverted principles,
“ Increase Aeshma and cruelty among the herd prosperers, 
“ By their tongues' conversation; themselves not being herd prosperers,
“ Whose own crimes, they do not overcome with good works.
“ These may yet establish the divinities of the Daevas,
“ Through the spirits of the Drujists, 
“ Away with them into the Dwelling of the Daevas,


“ May zeal and fat food reward him, who, in spirit, communes with Vohu Manah,
“ Because he is well informed about armaiti through asha;
“ O’ Mazdah!
“ I judge all these belong into thy realm!s”


Successor’s Call:


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah,
“ I beg you and Asha to declare your wisdom's double purposes
“ In connection with the proper solution of the problem,
“ How to obtain a ready hearing for the spirit of one like you.


“ Hear, O’ Vohu Manah!
“ Hear, O’ Asha! 
“ Hearken thou, O’ Ahura Mazdah!


“ What peer, what gentleman; whose influence would enforce regulations?
“ Who will initiate regulations,
“ Whose observance would earn a good reputation,
“ For the group of those who labour at herding cattle?


“ I beg of thee,
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah
“ To grant, to Frashaoshtra, the most blissful communion with Asha;
“ And to me,
“ To attain the good in the Xshathra
“ While both of us wish to be thy messengers, forever in the age 
“ Let the prosperer, the Ashaist believer,
“ Who was shaped and formed to profit the world,
“ Listen to the teachings!
         
“ Let not him, who utters or possesses the just words,
“ Have any communion with the Drujists.
“ Let the spirits occupied in search of the best compensation join together.
“ Let both the man, Zarathushtra and Jamaspa, unite with Asha.


“ Whereupon, O’ Ahura Mazdah,
“ Zarathushtra will commit to thy dwelling as treasury of merit of the mindful divinity,
“ The memory of the good thoughts, souls, and adorations,
“ With the zeal and armaiti devotions of the Ashaists,
“ All of which, O’ great ruler of Xshathra,
“ Mayest thou guard with enduring energy.


“ But, on the other hand, 
“ The evil man, the psychopompic souls, carrying foods, 
“ Will go to meet the bad administration,
“ Bad deeds, bad speeches, and bad spirits of the evil minded Drujists,
“ Who will be, genuine, worthy and accepted companions
“ For the inmates of the Dwelling of the Druj! 


“ O’ Asha! 
“ What sort of help hast thou for me, who am calling to thee?
“ With Vohu Manah, what help hast thou for me?


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, with praises do I sue for thy friendship,
“ Praying for whatever is best among your compensating rewards!”


Drujist Restraints:


“ Toward what earth shall I turn to flee? Whither shall I turn to escape?
“ The groups of the herdsmen do not offer me the satisfaction of support;
“ Nobody offers me gifts on the part of gentleman or of peer;
“ Neither do those who are princes of the land favour me,
“ Because of the Drujist;


“ How shall I ever please thee, by successful establishment of thy cult,
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah? 


“ I know, the reason why I am so despised as to be impotent
“ It is only because I possess so few flocks and followers!
“ I complain of this to thee!
“ Investigate this complaint, and then support me as friendship would dictate!
“ But, I specially ask instruction, as to how to attain, through asha,
“ The possession of vohu manah?




“ When, O’ Ahura Mazdah, will those bully days arrive?


“ When humanity's life will attain the blessings of Asha,
“ Through the growing teachings of saviours, who will be men of understanding?
“ Who are they, to whom Zarathushtra will come, with Vohu Manah, to help?
“ For myself I choose this mission, to fulfill it, with thy aid, 
“ O’ Ahura!


“ But, the Drujist is restraining those who are a prospering of asha,
“ Prospering the interests of the cattle, or of the village, or of the province;
“ Being notorious, the Drujist is repulsive through his own deeds,
“ So much so, that whoever may despatch him from the xshathra of life,
“ He will be doing the community a service,
“ And, be going on the progressing paths of good teachings.”
How to Treat Converts:


“ Whatever well informed person may have managed to withhold an Ashaist, or Drujist,
“ From keeping his vows or engagements, that are evil,
“ So as to come to a decision to live according to the divine observances,


“ That above well informed person
“ Should give due public notice of that conversion to some gentleman,
“ So that this gentleman might from then on protect the convert
“ From further maltreatment.


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah,


“ But, whoever, when thus warned of the converts conversion,
“ Shall not go to his side to stand up for him,
“ He shall be banished to the genuine houses of the Druj.
“ He will be accounted a genuine Druj.
“ Just as a Drujist is most attractive to the Drujist,
“ While the Ashaist is dear to whomsoever is an Ashaist
“ Since the time when thou createdst the first spirits.
“ O’ Ahura Mazdah!”


With Hostility:


“ Whom, O’ Mazdah, wilt thou commission as guardian for one like me,
“ Whenever the Drujist prepares to commit violence against me? 
“ Unless he be some one of divine power like thee,
“ Such as thy fire and manah,
“ Through the deeds of both of whom Asha is ripened for the consummation.
“ O’ Ahura: 
“ This is the teaching I propose to proclaim to human spirits. 


“ May not misfortune attain me,
“ Through the deeds of any one who intends to commit violence against my substance,
“ Rather, may his deeds, with hostility, rebound on his own body,
“ And restrain him from good life. 


“ O’ Mazdah,


“ With hostility, fail to restrain him from the bad life!“


The Second Life Of Man:


“ Who is that faithful, who will be the first one to teach me, by object lesson,
“ To consider thee the most helpful divinity,
“ And the most bounteous lord, as judge over deeds?


“ On the contrary,
“ The believers are, with the aid of Vohu Manah,
“ Seeking to hear from me!
“ What Asha uttered to thee,
“ And, what the Shaper of the Cattle uttered to Asha. 


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, MindfulLlord,
“ Grant Xshathra, the power of the coming kingdom, through Vohu Manah
“ As a compensation to doers of right;


“ Namely, 
“ Whatever any man or woman may contribute or give to me,
“ Do thou, O’ Lord, reward with what spiritual gifts thou knowest to be best
“ For life, for people;
“ And, I will also urge these rewarded people to adoration of one such as you
“ When I precede, all of these rewarded contributors,
“ On to the Sifter’s Bridge of Judgment. 




“ Through their political power,
“ The Karapans will join the Kavays in destroying the second life of men
“ Through evil deeds.


“ Whereas, their own souls and spirits shall terrify them
“ When they shall come to the Sifter's Bridge of Judgment!
“ As if companions, shall they be admitted to the home of the Druj, forever! 


“ When, through the help of Asha among the vigorous, 
“ Closer relatives and more distant relatives of the friendly Turas,
“ There shall arise, such as will, with zeal proper, the substance of Armaiti;
“  Then shall Ahura Mazdah, with the aid of Vohu Manah, 
“ In the consummation, give support to these friendly Turas.


“ Whoever satisfies Spitama Zarathushtra through preparations for him
“ Among men, he is worthy to be heard of;
“ And besides, he shall be given life or people by Ahura Mazdah;
“ And shall have his substance prospered through Vohu Manah.
“ And, he shall be considered well befriended with Asha.”


Return to the Steppes:


“ Would you like to know who is one of these, namely, a friendly Ashaist?
“ One who would like to be heard from for the Magian cause
“ Along with Zarathushtra in the crisis of political establishment?


“ It is Kavay Vishtaspa!


“ He is, O’ Ahura Mazdah,
“ One of those whom thou wilt surely unite to thyself
“ In thy same abode of praise, Garodman;  
“ Them, from among the people am I inviting with utterances of vohu manah, 
        
“ O’ Frashaoshtra Hvogva, 
“ Go thou with the faithful to where we both desire to be,
“ Namely, with those who are beloved;
“ Where Armaiti’s well being follows along with Asha,
“ Where are found the treasures of Vohu Manah,
“ And where Ahura Mazdah dwells and prospers the Xshathra.”


Promised Paradise:


“ I will inform you, O’ you clansmen of Haechataspa, and of Spitama,
“ How to sift the clever from the foolish:


“ With these deeds you maintain for yourselves, Asha,
“ Through the first decrees of Ahura.
“ So that in this place of trouble, O’ Jamaspa Hvogva,
“ I am uttering for all of your utterances, not of indifferent matters,
“ But praises of the preparer, 


“ Zarathushtra himself, who will have stood for you, together with Sraosha
“ Where I shall mention your misfortunes only, so as to arouse Mazdah's pity,
“ Where I shall sing in metred, and unmetred lines
“ Who shall sift the clever from the fools, through Asha,
“ His clever advisory manager.


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah! 
“ This is the sifting conclusion of my understanding, and of my mind: 


“ Whoever to me concedes the best our age,
“ Affords in the way of recognition or contributions, 
“ To him would I, by teaching, promise,the best treasures of my wealth,
“ With the vohu manah.


“ But, I will oppose hate to those who hate us,
“ And in doing so, I consider, 
“ O’ Mazdah, 
“ That I am, through Asha, satisfying your will


“ Whoever, through the inspiration of Asha
“ May genuinely cooperate with me, Zarathushtra, 
“ Doing those deeds which suit my will,
“ These deeds will earn for him future compensation;
“ And with all other expected rewards in paradise,
“ A pair of calving cows on the pasture of Asha,
“ And mayest thou, O’ Mazdah, effect these promises to those whom they best suit!” 


Hope:


“ When Zarathushtra shall overcome the Druj by retributions, through asha,
“ When he arrives at what long since was announced, 
“ Through the fate of Daevas and the doomed, in the Realm of Immortality,
“ Then, O’ Ahura,
“ Zarathushtra will, with profits, prosper the faithful believer who prays to thee.


“ Speak to me, O’ Ahura, 
“ For thou art an expert-knower!
“ Will the Ashaist overcome the Drujist in this world,
“ Before the beginning of the compensations, which thou hast thought out?


“ That would be a good message to the world!”


Expert-knower:


“ But, for an expert-knower, that is the best of teachings,
“ Which the beneficent Ahura is teaching, with Asha.
“ The expert-knower who is one like thee,
“ O’ Mazdah, through the understanding of Vohu Manah,
“  Knows bounteous secrets which are expressed in the doctrines


“ Whoever, O’ Mazdah, exercises his mind now through the better,
“ And then again through the worse meditations,
“ Whoever exercises his spirit with such uncertain deed and word,
“ Whoever follows the delights, wishes, and convictions of such uncertainty,
“ Shall he, in thy estimation, at last be separated from those who have done thy will?
“ Shall he, in thy estimation, 
“ At last be separated from those who are worthy of being in several places? 
“ Shall he, in thy estimation,
“ At last be separated from those who are becoming confused?”


Good Dwelling:


“ May not bad rulers rule over us!


“ Rather, may good rulers rule, with deeds inspired by good doctrinal thoughts,
“ O’ Armaiti, thou best one,
“ Who, for mortal men, dost perfect an additional, later, especial birth,
“ But for the cattle, perfecting that pasturage which should fatten it for our food,


“ O’ Armaiti, prized by men of Vohu Manah,


“ For men, she produced good dwelling, enduringness, and vitality.
“ But for that cattle, Ahura Mazdah with Asha,
“ Caused plants to grow at the birth of the first life.


“ Let Aeshma be put down; oppose cruelty!
“ Ye’ who wish to assure yourselves of the reward of Vohu Manah,
“ Through Asha, 
“ To whose bond would wish to belong, 
“ The bounteous man who shall abide in thy dwelling,
“ O’ Ahura!”


Wealth of Thy Good Xshathra


“ O’ Ahura Mazdah, is the wealth of thy good xshathra meant for me,
“ Is the wealth of thy compensation meant for me?


“ Will thy realm and compensation please the faithful believer
“ When it shall have been made manifest through Asha?


“ This would indeed be an incitation or, weighing off of the deeds of the good mentality! 


“ When may I know, O’ Ahura Mazdah,
“ Whether thou rulest, through Asha,
“ Over every one from whom destruction threatens me?


“ It is no-more-than right that thou shouldst tell me, in just words,
“ The decrees of Vohu Manah. 


“ For  I, who am the saviour
“ Would like to know how his compensation shall be given to him?”


Conquerors of Aeshma:


“ When, O’ Ahura Mazdah, will men gain knowledge of the message?
“ When will they expel the dregs of this haoma intoxication?
“ Whereby, is practised deceit by the hostile Karapans,
“ And the badland rulers, who are in the position to understand. 


“ When, O’ Mazdah, will Armaiti with Asha, and Xshathra, and good-dwelling,
“ Come, ye’, to us and eventuate in a pastoral realm?


“  Who will give us peace, through bloody slaughter of the Drujists?


“ Unto whom will the doctrinal thought of Vohu Manah enter and with him abide?


“ O’ Mazdah, these are the saviours of lands, 
“ Who, with Vohu Manah and Asha,
“ Strive to satisfy thy teachings or doctrines through deeds.


“ Yet, they become conquerors of Aeshma!”

"Anzar/Anshar ð’€­ð’Š¹ - The deification of the Heavens and the sky, as well as the deification of outer space

"Anu ð’€­ð’€­/Banebdjedet/ - The son of Anshar who deposed Alalu from his throne and withdrew the Enshi gods from war between tribes after the death of Bunri.
                                       The deification of the sky
Anu  etymology: Sumerian ""Sky/Heaven""
Banebdjedet etymology: Egyptian ""Ba the Lord of Djedet""",
" Nudimmud ð’‰¡ð’´ð’„·ð’„­/ð’€­ð’‰¡ð’¶ð’„·ð’„­/Enki ð’€­ð’‚—ð’†  /Ea ð’€­ð’‚ð’€€/Ea-sarru/Ptah/Kothar wa-HasisðŽ‹ðŽ˜ðŽ—ðŽ†ðŽƒðŽ’ðŽ’/Horon ðŽˆðŽ—ðŽ/Hayani/Hayyin/Ptah-tanen/Ahura Mazdah - Adopted by Anu after the death of his father and raised as brother to Enlil. 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                     The god of wisdom, craftsmenship,creation, and water.
Nudimmud etymology: Sumerian meaning ""Creator""/ Lit. translation ""the One Not Bound by Blood/Fear""/ euphamism for Curious?
Enki etymology: Sumerian ""Lord of the Earth/Territory""
Ea etymology: Sumerian ""Father of the House of Water""
Ptah etymology: Ancient Egyptian ""pth"" meaning: ""The Maker/Creator""
Horon etymology: Ancient Egyptian ""hwrwn"" meaning: ""Unknown""
Hayani/Hayyin etymology: Unknown, possibly Hurrian ""hyn"", Semetic(West) ""hyy""(""Life""), or Arabic ""hayyinun"" (""easy"") meaning: Unknown
Ptah-tanen etymology: Ancient Egyptian ""ttnn/ttnj"" meaning: ""Who Raised/Distinguished the Land/Mound""
Ahura Mazdah etymology: Avestan meaning: ""Lord of Wisdom""",Abzu ð’ªð’€Š/ð’ªð’€Šð’† /Engur ð’‡‰/Apsu/Apsuwa ð’€Šð’‹¢ - The Knowing Sea/Sea of Knowing 
"Nunamnir ð’‰¡ð’‰†ð’‰ª /Enlil (Ellil) ð’€­ð’‚—ð’†¤/Bel ð’€­ð’‚—/El ðŽ›ðŽ/Elkunirsa ð’‚–ð’†ªð’‰Œð’…•ð’Š­/Osiris/Onnophris - Raised as the firstborn of Anu and established as Prince of the Gods. For a time he was king of the Anuna-gods, before Enki was given the role.
                                                                                                                        The deification of the wind and air
Nunamnir etymology: Sumerian ""the One Who Decides Destinies""/""the One Without Fate""
Enlil etymology: Uknown/Sumerian "" the Lord of the Wind"", exact meaning Unknown
Bel etymology: Akkadian word for ""lord"", translates to ""the Lord""
El etymology: Ugaritic word for ""lord"", translates to ""the Lord""
Elkunirsa etymology: Hittite, direct translation Unknown
Osiris etymology: Borrowed from Egyptian ""wjsr"", ""The Powerful""
Onnophris etymology: Borrowed from Egyptian  ""wnn-nfrw"", ""He Who is Good""/""He who is Happy""(in a state of well-being)",Eridug/ Eridu ð’‰£ð’†  -Land/Country/State of the Prince
"Nisaba/Nidaba/Nissaba/ ð’€­ð’Šºð’‰€/ Nanibgal ð’€­ð’€­ð’‰€/Nun-bar-ce-gunu ð’€­ð’‰£ð’‡ð’Šºð’„–ð’‰¡/ð’€­ð’‰£ð’‡ð’Šºð’¯ð’‰¡/Renenut -  First-born of Enki and Ninhursag. She served as cheif scribe to Anu and is Enlil's mother-in-law.
                                                                                                                                The Goddess of Grain and Writing.
Nisaba etymology: Unknown, it is believed the name translates to ""Lady of Grain"" but her name is composed of the logogram for grain and potash
Nun-bar-ce-gunu etymology: Unknown, meaning: Unknown
Nidaba etymology: Unknown, alternate spelling of Nisaba
Nissaba  etymology: Unknown, alternate spelling of Nisaba
Renenut etymology: Egyptiam, approx. meaning: ""the Nurse""",E-engur ð’‚ð’‡‰/Sea-House/E-engura (temple of the Abzu) ð’‚ð’‡‰ð’Š - The temple of the Abzu built by Enki
"Haya ð’€­ð’„©ð’‰Œ- An official and solidier of Enlil, father of Sud, and husband of Nisaba.
           etymology: Unknown, partial translation ""Glistening (Some kind of animal(assumed to aquatic or a fish))""","Hal-an-kug/Halan-Kug ð’„¬ð’€­ð’†¬- meaning and etymology unclear. possible logographic meaning ""The Distribution of Holy/Pure/[Silver(as a quality of shining)?] Heaven"", ""The Distribution of Heaven's Holiness/Purity/[Metal?""], ""The Basket of Holy/Pure/[Silver(as a quality of shining)?] Heaven"", ""The Basket of Heaven's Holiness/Purity/Metal"", ""The Pure/Silver Basket of Heaven"", ""The Pure/Silver(as a quality of shining)? Distribution of Heaven"""
"Sud  ð’€­ð’‹¢ð’†³ð’Š’/ð’€­ð’‹¢ð’†³ð’Š’ð’† /Ninlil ð’€­ð’Žð’†¤/Nintud ð’€­ð’Žð’Œ…/Elat/Athiratu/Athirat ðŽ€ðŽ˜ðŽ—ðŽš/ð’€€ð’…†ð’‹¥/×Ö²×©Öµ××¨Ö¸×”/ð’€€ð’Šºð’…•ð’Œˆ/Isis - Wife of Enlil and daughter of Nisaba and Haya. She was regarded as a princess and later queen who determined fates along with Enlil
Sud etymology: Unknown, 
Ninlil etymology: Uknown/Sumerian "" the Lady of the Wind"", exact meaning Unknown
Nintud etymology: Sumerian "" the Lady of Birth-giving""
Elat etymology: Ugaritic for ""Lady""
Athiratu etymology: Ugaritic, meaning :Unknown
Athirat etymology: Canaanite, meaning :Unknown
Isis etymology: Greek version of Egyptian ""êœ£st"", approx. meaning : ""Queen of the Throne""","TukriÅ¡ ð’Œ…ð’ŠŒð’Š‘ð’…–ð’†  - possibly logographic meaning ""Land Born/Created by the Lion and Imposed with Dunes"", ""The Land Imposed With Dunes That Gives Birth To Lions"""
"Anuna/Anunnaki ð’€­ð’€€ð’‰£ð’ˆ¾/ The Ennead -The great judges of Heaven, the Earth, and the Underworld
Anuna etymology: Sumerian, approx. meaning: ""the Offspring of An"" 
Anunnaki etymology: Akkadian variant of Anuna,
Ennead etymology: from Greek ""á¼Î½Î½Î­Î±"", meaning ""nine""","á¸ªarali ð’„©ð’Šð’‡·  - possibly logographic meaning ""Where the Fish Drive Down Upon the Junipers"""
"Nuska ð’€­ð’‰ºð’Œ†/Nusku ð’€­ð’‰ºð’‡» - The vizier of Enlil. It is unclear whether Enlil is the biological father of Nuska, as well as ""the Father"" of Nuska through being the leader/king of his own house/domain. 
                                      Nuska was often associated with Gibil, and it is plausible the pair are father and son.

The nature of her marriage to Anu is unknown and there is dispute to which, if any, of her chidlren with Anu are not actually fathered by Enki.

"Ickur/Ishkur/Adad/Addu/Hadad/Hadd ( ðŽ…ðŽ„)ð’€­ð’…Ž/Tessub ð’€­ð’…Ž/ð’€­ð’‹¼ð’Œð’‹¢ð’Œ’(ðŽšðŽ˜ðŽ)/KA.ZAL ð’ŽŽð’…—ð’Š©ð’†·(KA.SAL.la)/NAM.HE ð’‰†ð’ƒ¶/Tarhun/Tarhunz/Marduk/Mar-Utu ð’€­ð’€«ð’Œ“/Rabb(the original ishkur/See Rabbim)/Baal ðŽðŽ“ðŽ â€¢ (bÊ¿l /baÊ¿lu/)
- Ishkur, later known as Adad or Hadad (from the root *hdd, ""to thunder"") - It is unknown what exactly happened to the original God of Storms, who was the son of Anu. Ninurta had earned the title for himself as King of the Igigi, before being stripped of the name Ninurta himself, at the hands of Enki.

Ickur/Ishkur etymology: Sumerian, meaning: Unknown
Adad/Addu/Hadad/Hadd  etymology: Proto-Semetic, meaning: ""Thunder""/""Thunderer""
Tessub etymology: Unknown, possibly Hurrian meaning: Unknown
KA.ZAL etymology: Unknown, possibly Sumerian meaning: Unknown, possibly ""The One Who Passed Through the Opening""
NAM.HE etymology: Unknown,possibly Sumerian meaning: exact Unknown, possibly a term refering to progeny
